plants.name: Pea Shooter
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>How can a single plant grow and shoot so many peas so quickly? Pea Shooter says, "Hard work, commitment, and a healthy, well-balanced breakfast of sunlight and high-fiber carbon dioxide make it all possible."</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Pea Shooters are your first line of defense. They shoot peas at attacking zombies.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>20/1.5s</color>
<color=black>Range: </color><color=#8B0000>Single lane</color></size>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>100</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>7.5s</color></size>

plants.name: Towards Sun Flower
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Towards Sun Flower can't resist bouncing to the beat. Which beat is that? Why, the life-giving jazzy rhythm of the Earth itself, thumping at a frequency only Towards Sun Flower can hear.</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Towards Sun Flowers are essential for you to produce extra sun. Try planting as many as you can!

<color=black>Sun Output: </color><color=#8B0000>25/25s</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>First production is 4~7s after planting.</color></size>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>50</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>7.5s</color></size>

plants.name: Cherry Peach Explode Bullet
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"I wanna explode," says Cherry #1. "No, let's detonate instead!" says his brother, Cherry #2. After intense consultation, they agree to explodonate.</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Cherry Peach Explode Bullets can blow up all zombies in an area. They have a short fuse so plant them near zombies.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>1800 (Cremator)</color>
<color=black>Range: </color><color=#8B0000>3×3 (A circle with a radius of 1.375 tiles)</color></size>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>150</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>50s</color></size>

plants.name: Tough Fruit
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"People wonder how I feel about getting constantly chewed on by zombies," says Tough Fruit. "What they don't realize is that with my limited senses, all I can feel is a kind of tingling, like a relaxing back rub."</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Tough Fruits have hard shells which protect your other plants.

<color=black>Toughness: </color><color=#8B0000>4000</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Tough Fruit and subsequent fusions prevent splash damage from explosive cherries at difficulty 4 or lower. </color></size>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>50</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>30s</color></size>

plants.name: Earth Bean Thunder
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Some folks say Earth Bean Thunder is lazy, that he leaves everything to the last minute. Earth Bean Thunder says nothing. He's too busy thinking about his investment strategy.</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Earth Bean Thunders pack a powerful punch, but they need a while to arm themselves. You should plant them ahead of zombies. They will explode on contact.

<color=black>Arming Time: </color><color=#8B0000>15~17s</color>
<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>1800</color>
<color=black>Range: </color><color=#8B0000>Single tile (A circle with a radius of 0.68 tiles)</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Short plant (evades straight-firing projectiles). </color></size>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>25</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>30s</color></size>

plants.name: Big Mouth Flower
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Big Mouth Flower almost got a gig doing stunts for "The Little Shop of Horrors" but it fell through when his agent demanded too much on the front end. Big Mouth Flower's not resentful, though. He says it's just part of the business.</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Big Mouth Flowers can devour a zombie whole, but they are vulnerable while chewing.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>Insta-kill (Devour)
<color=black>Digestion Time: </color><color=#8B0000>30s</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Deals 40 damage each bite to zombies that are immune to being devoured.</color></size>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>150</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>7.5s</color></size>

plants.name: Little Spray Mushroom
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"I only recently became aware of the existence of zombies," says Little Spray Mushroom. "Like many fungi, I'd just assumed they were fairy tales or movie monsters. This whole experience has been a huge eye-opener for me."</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Little Spray Mushrooms are cheap, but can only fire a short distance.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>20/1.5s</color>
<color=black>Range: </color><color=#8B0000>2.5 tiles ahead</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Can stack up to three times in a single tile.
<color=black>•</color> Little Spray Mushroom fusions can only be created by planting the other ingredient on Little Spray Mushroom(s), and this will also attempt to fuse with other plants on the same tile (Pots, South Melons).
<color=black>•</color> Short plant (evades straight-firing projectiles).</color>
<color=black>Spreading: </color><color=#8B0000>Plant Big Spray Mushroom or Little Courage Mushroom on 1/2/3 Little Spray Mushrooms to spread it to empty tiles ahead/in a fan shape, spawning 1/3/6 Little Spray Mushrooms.</color></size>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>0</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>7.5s</color></size>

plants.name: Big Spray Mushroom
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"I was in a dead-end job producing yeast spores for a bakery," says Big Spray Mushroom. "Then Little Spray Mushroom, bless 'im, told me about this great opportunity blasting zombies. Now I really feel like I'm making a difference."</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Big Spray Mushrooms shoot fumes that can pass through screen doors.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>20/1.5s (Piercing)</color>
<color=black>Range: </color><color=#8B0000>4 tiles ahead</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Has infinite pierce, ignores handheld armor (Type 2).
<color=black>•</color> Little Spray Mushroom spreader.</color></size>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>75</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>7.5s</color></size>

plants.name: Charm Confuse Mushroom
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"Rigid Corpses are our friends," asserts Charm Confuse Mushroom. "They're badly misunderstood creatures who play a valuable role in our ecology. We can and should do more to bring them round to our way of thinking."</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>When eaten, Charm Confuse Mushrooms will make a zombie turn around and fight for you.

<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Makes a single zombie fight for you.</color></size>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>75</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>30s</color></size>

plants.name: Little Courage Mushroom
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"Who's there?" whispers Little Courage Mushroom, voice barely audible. "Go away. I don't want to see anybody. Unless it's the man from the circus."</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Little Courage Mushrooms are long-ranged shooters that hide when enemies get near them.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>20/1.5s</color>
<color=black>Range: </color><color=#8B0000>Single lane</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Gets scared and stops attacking when there are zombies in the 3×3 surrounding area.
<color=black>•</color> Little Spray Mushroom spreader.</color></size>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>25</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>7.5s</color></size>

plants.name: Cold Ice Mushroom
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Cold Ice Mushroom frowns, not because he's unhappy or because he disapproves, but because of a childhood injury that left his facial nerves paralyzed.</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Cold Ice Mushrooms temporarily immobilize all zombies on the screen.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>20</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Freezes all zombies in place for 4 seconds, inflicts Chill for 10 seconds and deals 20 damage to all non-flying zombies.</color></size>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>75</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>50s</color></size>

plants.name: Destroy Eradicate Mushroom
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"You're lucky I'm on your side," says Destroy Eradicate Mushroom. "I could destroy everything you hold dear. It wouldn't be hard."</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Destroy Eradicate Mushrooms destroy everything in a large area and leave a crater that can't be planted on.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>1800 (Cremator)</color>
<color=black>Range: </color><color=#8B0000>5×5 (A circle with a radius of 3.75 tiles)</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>After explosion, leaves a crater that lasts for 180 seconds. Does not leave crater when planted on water.</color></size>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>125</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>50s</color></size>

plants.name: Sleep Lily
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Sleep Lily never complains. Sleep Lily never wants to know what's going on. Put a plant on top of Sleep Lily, he won't say a thing. Does he have startling opinions or shocking secrets? Nobody knows. Sleep Lily keeps it all inside.</color></size>
plants.info: Sleep Lilys let you plant non-aquatic plants on top of them. Special: Short plant. Non-aquatic plants can be planted on it. When specific plants are placed, Sleep Lily transforms into a variant of it that can automatically plant the plant that transformed it every 90 seconds. Variants: Towards Sun Flower, Tough Fruit, 3 Line Shooter, Squash Melon, Fire Torch Tree Stump, Fire Burst Spicy Pepper.
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>25</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>7.5s</color></size>

plants.name: Squash Melon
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"I'm ready!" yells Squash Melon. "Let's do it! Put me in! There's nobody better! I'm your guy! C'mon! Whaddya waiting for? I need this!"</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Squash Melones will smash the first zombie that gets close to it.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>1800 (Squash Melon)</color>
<color=black>Detection Range: </color><color=#8B0000>0.5 tiles to the left; 1 tile to the right</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Does damage in a small area with a radius of 0.37 tiles. Squash Meloned zombies do not trigger their on-death effects.</color></size>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>50</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>30s</color></size>

plants.name: 3 Line Shooter
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>3 Line Shooter likes reading, backgammon, and long periods of immobility in the park. 3 Line Shooter enjoys going to shows, particularly modern jazz. "I'm just looking for that special someone," he says. 3 Line Shooter's favorite number is 5.</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>3 Line Shooters shoot peas in three lanes.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>20×3/1.5s</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>3 Line Shooter and subsequent fusions redirect projectiles that would be lost off-map on topmost/bottommost lanes to their own lane.</color></size>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>275</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>7.5s</color></size>

plants.name: Tangle Surround Water Grass
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"I'm totally invisible," Tangle Surround Water Grass thinks to himself. "I'll hide here just below the surface, and nobody will see me." His friends tell him they can see him perfectly well, but he'll never change.</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Tangle Surround Water Grass are aquatic plants that pull the first zombie that nears them underwater.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>Insta-kill (Drown)</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Short plant (evades straight-firing projectiles). </color></size>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>25</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>50s</color></size>

plants.name: Fire Burst Spicy Pepper
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"NNNNNGGGGG...." Fire Burst Spicy Pepper says. He's not going to explode, not this time. But soon. Oh, so soon. It's close. He knows it; he can feel it; his whole life's been leading up to this moment.</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Fire Burst Spicy Peppers destroy an entire row of zombies.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>1800 (Cremator)</color>
<color=black>Range: </color><color=#8B0000> Single lane</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Instantly eliminates Snow Sled Vehicle Rigid Corpses and their ice trails.</color></size>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>125</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>50s</color></size>

plants.name: Ground Spike
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Hockey is Ground Spike's obsession. He's got box seat season tickets. He keeps close track of his favorite players. And he consistently cleans up in the office hockey pool. Just one problem: he's terrified of pucks.</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Ground Spikes pop tires and hurt any zombies that step on them.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>20/1 Second</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Short plant (evades straight-firing projectiles).
<color=black>•</color> Does not block regular zombies.
<color=black>•</color> Pops tires. Instantly kills Snow Sled Vehicle Rigid Corpses. If Snow Sled Ice Vehicle Rigid Corpse or Dance King Ice Vehicle Rigid Corpse has more than 50% max HP, damage is capped at 50% of their max HP. If Evolved Snow Sled Vehicle Rigid Corpses have more than 1/3 toughness, damage is capped at 1/3 of their max HP.</color></size>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>100</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>7.5s</color></size>

plants.name: Fire Torch Tree Stump
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Everybody likes and respects Fire Torch Tree Stump. They like him for his integrity, for his steadfast friendship, for his ability to greatly maximize pea damage. But Fire Torch Tree Stump has a secret: He can't read.</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Fire Torch Tree Stumps turn peas that pass through them into fireballs that deal twice as much damage.

<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Ignites peas that pass through it, damage +20.
<color=black>•</color> Turns snow peas back into regular peas. 
<color=black>•</color> Dispels fog in a 1-tile radius.</color></size>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>175</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>7.5s</color></size>

plants.name: Sea Mushroom
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Sea Mushroom has never seen the sea. It's in his name, he's heard loads about it, but he's just never found the time. One day, though, it'll go down.</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Sea Mushrooms are aquatic plants that shoot short ranged spores.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>20/1.5s</color>
<color=black>Range: </color><color=#8B0000> 2.5 tiles ahead</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Short plant (evades straight-firing projectiles).
<color=black>•</color> Returns 50 sun after fusions, except Sea Mushroom Group (with Little Spray Mushroom).</color></size>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>0</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>7.5s</color></size>

plants.name: Road Lantern Flower
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Road Lantern Flower defies science. He just does. Other plants eat light and excrete oxygen; Road Lantern Flower eats darkness and excretes light. Road Lantern Flower's cagey about how he does it. "I'm not gonna say 'sorcery,' I wouldn't use the term 'dark forces,' I just... I think I've said enough."</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Road Lantern Flowers light up an area to let you see through fog, and give Lumos levels to plants.

<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Dispels fog (5×7 except the corners). Road Lantern Flower fusions dispel fog in a smaller 1-tile radius.</color>
<color=#99582a>Lumos System: </color><color=#8B0000>Provides 1 Lumos Level in a 3×3 area, which can be stacked to provide strength to other Lumos plants. Refer to its fusions for the list of affected plants.</color></size>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>25</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>15s</color></size>

plants.name: Sage Person Palm
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>She's prickly, sure, but Sage Person Palm' spikes belie a spongy heart filled with love and goodwill. She just wants to hug and be hugged. Most folks can't hang with that, but Sage Person Palm doesn't mind. She's been seeing an armadillo for a while and it really seems to be working out.</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Cacti shoot spikes that can hit both ground and air targets.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>20/1.5s</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Prioritizes flying zombies.</color></size>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>125</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>7.5s</color></size>

plants.name: 3 Leaf Grass
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>When 3 Leaf Grass was five he got a shiny new birthday cake. 3 Leaf Grass made his wish, huffed and puffed, but was able to extinguish only 60% of the candles. Instead of giving up, though, he's used that early defeat as a catalyst to push himself harder ever since.</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>3 Leaf Grasss blow away all balloon zombies and fog, except Iron Bucket-copter Rigid Corpses and Fly Ship Rigid Corpses.

<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Clears fog for 30s. Blows away Air Ball Rigid Corpses and Thunder Cry Machine Instrument Dive Ship Rigid Corpse missiles. Pushes back Fly Ship Rigid Corpses slightly.</color></size>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>100</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>15s</color></size>

plants.name: Foreign Peach
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"Aw, man," says Foreign Peach. "I went to the dentist the other day, and he said that I have four cavities. I've got—count it—ONE tooth! Four cavities in one tooth? How does this happen?"</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Foreign Peachs shoot stars in 5 directions.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>20×5/1.5s</color></size>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>125</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>7.5s</color></size>

plants.name: South Melon
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>South Melon had just wrapped up his part-time gig. "Honestly, it was a pretty fun experience," he chuckled. "But can someone explain why they kept stuffing candy and candles inside me?"</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>South Melons protect plants that are within their shells.

<color=black>Toughness: </color><color=#8B0000>4000</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>South Melon and subsequent fusions prevent splash damage from explosive cherries at difficulty 4 or lower. Use a South Melon seed packet on South Melon and his fusions with less than 2/3 Toughness to restore HP to full.</color>
<color=black>Fusion Priority: </color><color=#8B0000>Lowest, hold Left Shift key while fusing to forcibly fuse with South Melon. (requires BetterFusionSuite mod, included by default)</color></size>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>125</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>30s</color></size>

plants.name: Magnet Force Mushroom
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Everyone wondered why Magnet Force Mushroom always took so long to "digest" the iron he absorbed, even after it had already been taken away for fusion. Frustrated, he snapped, "I'm not lazy! I just got knocked out by flying iron!"</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Magnet Force Mushrooms can absorb metallic objects.

<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Attracts metal objects within a radius of 3 tiles. Takes 15 seconds to process. Drops unprocessed objects on death or fusion.</color>
<color=black>If there is a magnetic plant in the 8-direction vicinity of another magnetic plant, they are then connected through the <color=#666699>Magnetic System</color>. The <color=#666699>Magnetic System</color> has no limit to its number of connections, and will boost the strength of some magnetic plants based on its amount. Refer to its fusions for the list of affected plants.</color></size>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>100</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>7.5s</color></size>

plants.name: Roll Heart Vegetable Thrower
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Some say love is like a cabbage; you have to peel it layer by layer to see the other person's heart. But Roll Heart Vegetable Thrower has some reservations about this, as he is still single himself.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Launches cabbages to attack zombies.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>40/3s</color>
<color=black>Range: </color><color=#8B0000>Single lane</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Lobbed projectiles ignore handheld armor (Type 2).</color></size>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>100</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>7.5s</color>

plants.name: Flower Pot
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Plants always think Flower Pot is just a "pot," but in reality, Flower Pot is a "flower" that just looks like a "pot." Look, that little sprout is the bud of a flower.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Allows planting plants above it.

<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Enables plants to be planted on bare ground/roof.
<color=black>•</color> Short plant (evades straight-firing projectiles).
<color=black>Fusion Priority: </color><color=#8B0000>Lowest, hold Left Alt key while fusing to forcibly fuse with Flower Pot. (requires BetterFusionSuite mod, included by default)</color>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>25</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>7.5s</color>

plants.name: Jade Rice Thrower
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Plants are curious where Jade Rice Thrower's butter comes from. Jade Rice Thrower doesn't know either, but that doesn't stop him from making popcorn with it in his spare time to earn a little extra cash.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Throws corn kernels to attack zombies and can also throw butter to immobilize them.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>20/3s</color>
<color=black>Range: </color><color=#8B0000>Single lane</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Has a 1/4 chance to throw butter, dealing 40 damage and immobilizing zombies for 4 seconds.<color=#8B0000></color>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>100</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>7.5s</color>

plants.name: Big Garlic
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Big Garlic boasts of his high allicin content, claiming that eating garlic is good for health. Whether that's true or not, you only have to watch the zombies gagging as they chew on him to know it's pretty effective. </color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Any zombie that tries to bite him will switch lanes.

<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Rigid Corpses that bite him will change lanes. Receives no more than 15 damage each bite.
<color=black>•</color> Short plant (evades straight-firing projectiles).</color>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>50</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>7.5s</color>

plants.name: Leaf Protect Guard Umbrella
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Once a renowned passer, Leaf Protect Guard Umbrella was a superstar in the 114th Plant-Rigid Corpse NBA. But as he aged, he discovered he was going bald.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Deflects incoming basketballs.

<color=black>Toughness: </color><color=#8B0000>1000</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Protects plants from catapult projectiles and Bungees within a 3×3 area.
<color=black>•</color> Protects plants from explosive basketballs but each protection costs 100 Toughness.
<color=black>•</color> Stops Machine Instrument Protect Age Ball Rigid Corpses in their tracks.</color>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>100</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>7.5s</color>

plants.name: Gold Goblet Flower
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Gold Goblet Flower is extraordinarily wealthy, so it spends freely—though technically, it doesn't even have hands or feet.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Produces Silver Coins, Gold Coins, and Diamonds.

<color=black>Production Interval: </color><color=#8B0000>25s</color>
<color=black>Coin Amount:
</color><color=#8B0000><color=black>•</color> 75% Chance = 100 Coins
<color=black>•</color> 20% Chance = 300 Coins
<color=black>•</color> 5% Chance = 1000 Coins</color>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>25</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>7.5s</color>

plants.name: West Melon Thrower
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>During breaks, West Melon Thrower loves to split its melon arm into two, nibbling away to cool off. "Want a taste?" it asks the zombies cheerfully, while the plants around, sweltering in the heat, can only drool enviously.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Launches melons at zombies to deal damage.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>80/3s</color>
<color=black>Range: </color><color=#8B0000>Single lane</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Melons deal splash damage in a 1-tile radius with falloff, calculated as projectile damage ×8 /(number of zombies hit), capped at 1/3 of the projectile's base damage.</color>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>300</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>7.5s</color>

plants.name: Steel Leaf Grass
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>For plants, spinning petals without damaging their stems is a skillful art. Some botanists speculate that Steel Leaf Grass might be distantly related to 3 Leaf Grass because of this ability. "It's indeed a tricky skill," Steel Leaf Grass said with a faux-profound air, "The real challenge lies in obtaining lightweight circular saws and painting them with petal patterns."</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Damages zombies in close range with his spinning sawblades!

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>8/0.02s</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Gains 5 points of Shield every 0.5s, up to 1×max HP points.
<color=black>•</color> Spins blades when zombies get close, dealing piercing damage that scales down with more targets, to a minimum of 1.</color>
<color=black>Shield Mechanic: </color><color=#8B0000>Shields provide a 20% damage reduction to protected plants.</color>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>100</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>7.5s</color>

plants.name: Flash Lightning Ocean Leek
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Flash Lightning Ocean Leek thought his moment had come — after all, who else had eaten lightning over a hundred times? Surely, he was destined to play the Frankenstein monster! But when he received the script, his dreams short-circuited. He wasn't a star. He wasn't even a sidekick. He was just Dr. Stanley's lightning rod...</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Attacks approaching zombies with lightning rays!

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>100/1.5s</color>
<color=black>Attack Range: </color><color=#8B0000>5×5</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Idle Flash Lightning Ocean Leeks in range will channel power to the attacking one, each boosting the damage by 150.
<color=black>•</color> Each shot can arc to other zombies to deal half the damage, up to a maximum of 3 zombies.</color>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>225</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>7.5s</color>

plants.name: Little Pine Furnace
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Little Pine Furnace won the sixth annual “Plant Helper” award of the Snowy Plains. Fellow plants agreed the honor was well deserved—after all, he’s so busy keeping others warm, he hasn’t even had time to clear the snow off his own head.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>A floating firwood heater that helps nearby plants resist the cold.

<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Hovering plant. Protects plants in its own tile from Snowballs and Blizzards.
<color=black>•</color> Deals 200 damage every 3s to ice blocks within its protection range.
<color=black>•</color> Absorbs the base Snow plant below it to temporarily evolve into Hi-temp Little Pine Furnace, expanding its protection range to 3×3 for (1.2 × Plant Cost) seconds before reverting. Otherwise lasts 180s by default.
<color=#cceaed>Snow Map Gimmick</color>:
<color=black>•</color> Ice blocks have 2000 HP and block straight-firing projectiles and zombies.
<color=black>•</color> If destroyed by plants, spawns the corresponding plants.
<color=black>•</color> If destroyed by zombies, spawns the corresponding zombotany instead; if none such zombotany exists, spawns an Snow Beast Rigid Corpse.
<color=black>•</color> A blizzard starts at the beginning of every wave, freezing all unprotected plants into ice blocks.</color>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>25</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>7.5s</color>

plants.name: Cloud Spruce Bower
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Cloud Spruce Bower just moved to the snowy plains. His pursed lips make him look fierce and hard to approach. But Little Pine Furnace isn't scared—he knows that expression is just from his new friend pulling up his collar because the snowfield is just that cold.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Cold-resistant archer that fires piercing spruce needles.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>20/1.5s</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Immune to freeze from Snowballs and Blizzards.
<color=black>•</color> Projectiles pierce once (including ice blocks) and ignore handheld armor (Type 2).</color>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>175</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>7.5s</color>

plants.name: Cold Ice Lily Flower
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Cold Ice Lily Flower never planned to become a healer—but when your mere presence makes nearby plants feel better, word spreads fast. Now she spends her days reluctantly running a clinic, insisting, “I’m just cold, not qualified.” Patients don’t seem to care.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Cold-loving healer that gains energy in the cold to heal nearby plants.

<color=#0077b6>Cryo System: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Gains 1 ice charge every 10s or when hit by a snowball or snow trident.
<color=black>•</color> Consumes 5 charges to awaken and heal all plants in a 3×3 area for 50 HP every 3s.</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Immune to freeze from Snowballs and Blizzards.</color>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>50</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>30s</color>

plants.name: Water Drop Aloe
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Water Drop Aloe thought opening a spa in the snowy plains would be relaxing—until she realized no one wants a cold soak in subzero weather. At least the snow keeps the towels crisp.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Cold-loving lobber that throws dew drops, or ice chunks when it's sufficiently chill.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>20/3s</color>
<color=#0077b6>Cryo System: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Gains 1/1/15 ice charges when affected by snowball/snow trident/Cold Ice Mushroom respectively.
<color=black>•</color> Consumes 1 charge to throw an ice chunk (×2 damage, applies Deep Chill, 40 Cryo points).
<color=black>•</color> Deep Chill: same as regular Chill but affects Frost Legionnaires too. Snow Sled Vehicle Rigid Corpses are still immune.
<color=black>•</color> Deals ×4 damage to frozen zombies.</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Immune to freeze from Snowballs.</color>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>75</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>7.5s</color>

plants.name: Winter Bamboo Road Block
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"Where there's a will, there’s a wall." That's Winter Bamboo Road Block's motto. "Back when I first sprouted, folks said I was just a pesky pebble underfoot. But I kept growing, and now I've become an official traffic cone." If you ask how long ago that was? "Three days," he says.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Damages incoming zombies at the cost of his own HP.

<color=black>Toughness: </color><color=#8B0000>1500</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Other plants cannot absorb damage for Winter Bamboo Road Blocks.
<color=black>•</color> Immune to crush.
<color=black>•</color> On contact with a zombie, instantly defeats the zombie if the zombie's HP is less than or equal to his own, but takes damage equal to that zombie's HP. Against zombies with higher HP, deals damage equal to his own HP to the zombie and destroys himself.</color>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>50</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>15s</color>

plants.name: Surprise Happy Yellow Gold Vase
plants.introduce: <color=#3D1400>Golden Recycling is the largest barter platform among plants. As the founder, Surprise Happy Yellow Gold Vase helps plants trade unused secondhand items, and also offers those looking for a random trip a chance, and a way, to get there.</color>
plants.info:
Recycle unwanted plants and turn them into surprises.

<color=#3D1400>Unlock Conditions: </color><color=red> Complete Vasebreaker: The Vase Less Travelled by, De-vase-tating! and Potluck</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> When charge is below 3, you can use the Glove to move plants inside and for it to gain charge. Base plants/simple fusions/advanced fusions grant 1/2/3 charges respectively
<color=black>•</color> Tap to break the pot. Drops a plant seed packet based on stored charge:
-1 charge: Base plant
-2 charge: Simple fusion
-3 or more: Advanced fusion

<color=black>•</color> If an Odyssey plant was ever stored, it will always drop an Odyssey plant.</color>
plants.cost:
Cost: <color=red>200</color>
Recharge: <color=red>30s</color>

plants.name: Fuse Together Movement Paint
plants.introduce: <color=#3D1400></color>
plants.info: Not a plant. It's an animation of base plants fusing into an Odyssey plant. You must have the setting "Easier Odyssey" enabled.

plants.name: Fierce Flame Burst Bamboo
plants.introduce: <color=#3D1400>As the New Year approaches, Fierce Flame Burst Bamboo hit the streets for some holiday shopping. "I used to be swamped every year, setting off fireworks at every house during the festival," he said. "But now, with these new firecrackers made from cherry-chilli infused gunpowder, I can finally relax and enjoy my retirement." It must be said that the new firecrackers are remarkably effective—he plans to buy a bundle this year too.</color>
plants.info:
Fierce Flame Burst Bamboo can scare off both zombies and demons, with a body as slender as a bundle of silk but breath as powerful as thunder.

<color=#3D1400>Unlock Conditions: </color><color=red> Complete Adventure Level 45</color>
<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>300</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> After being planted, waits for 1s before flying forward in his lane, knocking back zombies it encounters and causing 300 area damage each impact.
<color=black>•</color> Deals ×15 damage to Nian zombies.</color>
plants.cost:
Cost: <color=red>275</color>
Recharge: <color=red>50s</color>

plants.name: Peaceful Safe Fruit
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"Peace and joy!" Peaceful Safe Fruit cheerfully greets every plant that passes by. Rigid Corpses get a special greeting instead: "In this season of gratitude, have a happy Halloween!", tailored based on the zombies' error-ridden calendar.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Wards off disaster and keeps you "apples" safe.

<color=#3D1400>Unlock Conditions: </color><color=red> Complete Adventure Level 45</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Starts with 0 energy. Max energy is capped at 4k. Regenerates 1 energy every 0.02s. When damaged or when energy is consumed, energy regen is paused for 1s. When energy is depleted, energy regen is paused for 5s.
<color=black>•</color> Consumes the amount of energy equal to the damage to block damage for plants in a 3×3 area.</color>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>150</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>50s</color>

plants.name: Iron Bucket
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Ever since the first bucket was made, it has found many unusual uses: armor, fusion materials, roadblocks, and more. Yet despite all that, it has never once been used as an actual bucket.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Can fuse with certain plants, making them hard as iron.

<color=#3D1400>Unlock Conditions: </color><color=red> Complete Adventure Level 45</color>
<color=black>Toughness: </color><color=#8B0000>2000</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Short plant (evades straight-firing projectiles).
<color=black>•</color> Can be placed (planted) on the ground like a normal plant, or used as a fusion ingredient.
<color=black>•</color> Plants with Iron Bucket in their recipe have higher toughness and take half damage from explosive cherries.</color>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>100</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>30s</color>

plants.name: Olive Hat
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"You can find these things everywhere on a football field," the plants say. But the Olive Machine Gun Shooter replies solemnly: "This helmet is special, because its former owner truly understood football."</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Can fuse with certain plants, making them hard as steel.

<color=#3D1400>Unlock Conditions: </color><color=red> Complete Adventure Level 45</color>
<color=black>Toughness: </color><color=#8B0000>4000</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Short plant (evades straight-firing projectiles).
<color=black>•</color> Can be placed (planted) on the ground like a normal plant, or used as a fusion ingredient.
<color=black>•</color> Plants with Olive Hat in their recipe have higher toughness and take half damage from explosive cherries.</color>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>200</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>50s</color>

plants.name: Lost Corpse Flower Pot
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>On second thought, it is rather unsettling: what makes the plants here grow so vigorously?</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>A Flower Pot with rich soil that boosts the damage of the plant growing on it.

<color=blue>Xiaoxiaosu Collab Plant, exclusive to Vasebreaker and Gacha Modes</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Enables plants to be planted on bare ground/roof.
<color=black>•</color> Short plant (evades straight-firing projectiles).
<color=black>•</color> Boosts the attack damage of the plants in its tile by +10%.</color>

plants.name: Diamond Rock Model Imitater
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>He is a merchant, the most stereotypical one. No stance, no loyalty, driven only by profit. He freely changes sides between plants and zombies, smuggling goods. What he brings you might be the key to a comeback… or complete trash… or even a powerful enemy. But what happens to you ultimately has nothing to do with him. After all, you knew exactly what you were buying.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>I'm shipping it out! - Wait, no, I've caused trouble again!

<color=#4A2900>Usage Condition: Odyssey Mode</color>
<color=#4A2900>Unlock Conditions: Complete Odyssey Gacha: Diamond Rock Model Imitater</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Only usable in Odyssey Gacha levels.
<color=black>•</color> 1.5s after appearing, generates a random plant, zombie, or event.
<color=black>•</color> Chances: Odyssey plants (10%); Plants obtainable from Giftboxes (50%); Odyssey zombies (10%); Bosses, Mini-bosses, or Odyssey zombies (10%); Common zombies (15%); Random events (5%). Generated zombies have random 0.8×/1×/1.2× health.
<color=black>•</color> List of Events: 
<color=black>-</color> 1. Gain 200 Sun.
<color=black>-</color> 2. Acquire an Odyssey Modifier.
<color=black>-</color> 3. Instantly kill all zombies within 1.5-tiles' range.
<color=black>-</color> 4. Deal 4000 damage to plants in a 3×3 area.
<color=black>-</color> 5. Create a column of Fireballs rolling right, inflicting Enflamed and leaving a 40-damage fire trail.</color>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>25</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>15s</color>

plants.name: Rigid Corpse Fire Red Lily
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"I'm done with all the pretentious rules of the garden. Plants should grow as they please," Rigid Corpse Fire Red Lily once told her companions, but the other plants didn't care. Maybe it's time to let go of all worries and live freely like a wild weed, she thought. "I'm not a plant, nor a zombie, nor some means of production. I am simply myself."</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>An exhausted Fire Red Lily that has lost her vitality, now as hard to remove as a weed.

<color=black>Toughness: </color><color=#8B0000>300</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Cannot be shoveled or moved with gloves. Ignored by Rigid Corpses.
<color=black>•</color> Absorbs plant-side projectiles that pass through it, taking 30 damage each time.
<color=black>•</color> 10s after appearance, summons a random Zombotany; summons another every 15s thereafter, and once more upon death.
<color=black>•</color> When fertilized, drops a Surprise Happy Gift Box and disappears.</color>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>-125</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>50s</color>

plants.name: Lucky Fate 4 Leaf Grass
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Lucky Fate 4 Leaf Grass never believed the number four was unlucky. She's convinced that with her cute looks and good fortune, she can help plants see the number four in a better light. Still, what plants really wonder is: what kind of wind could possibly change the inside of a tightly sealed box?</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Changes the winds of fate for you~

<color=#3D1400>Unlock Conditions: </color><color=red> Complete Adventure Level 45</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Forces your next Surprise Happy Gift Box to generate a guaranteed Advanced/Titan fusion. In Odyssey Gacha Mode, it will instead guarantee an Odyssey plant.</color>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>200</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>50s</color>

plants.name: Glow Fire Sun Light Mushroom · Glow Light Glitter Gem
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>The first time Lumo-shroom saw a lumofly as a child, she was captivated. The flickering lights in the grass: a life so beautiful yet fleeting, like lamps or stars, painting the night sky with dreams. Perhaps that's why she looks at the lumo-suns she produces with joy instead of distaste.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Sun Light Mushroom living in symbiosis with lumoflies.

<color=blue>PvZ: Symbiosis Collab Plant</color>
<color=black>Sun Output: </color><color=#8B0000>20→35/25s
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Sun Light Mushrooms convert into Lumo-shrooms, gaining the <u>Lumo-sun</u> ability after 150s if their tile's Lumos Level is 1 or higher. Each additional Lumos Level accelerates the conversion by 100%.
<color=black>•</color> Lumo-sun: Produces Lumo-sun, which is worth 20 each (35 when grown up).</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Sun Light Mushroom + Lumos Level</color>

plants.name: Glow Fire Sun Light Mushroom · Glow Fire Flutter Follow
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>It wasn't until she heard the story of "decayed grass giving birth to lumoflies" that Lumo-shroom understood why her friends never seemed sad when parting. To them, each cycle was a grand reunion, not the end of life. "We are destined to part, but we are also destined to meet again."</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Second form of Lumo-shroom.

<color=blue>PvZ: Symbiosis Collab Plant</color>
<color=black>Sun Output: </color><color=#8B0000>20→35/25s
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> After an additional 150s under Lumos, Lumo-shrooms gain the <u>Lumo-sprite</u> ability. Each additional Lumos Level accelerates upgrades speed by 100%.
<color=black>•</color> Lumo-sprite: When a zombie bites a Lumo-shroom, 1 Lumo-sprite starts to follow that zombie (2 if grown up). Each time a followed zombie takes damage, 1 Lumo-sprite flies off and produces a small Lumo-sun. When the zombie dies, all Lumo-sprites following it are released, dropping an equal number of Lumo-suns. A zombie can have up to 20 Lumo-sprites following it at once.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Lumo-shroom + Lumos Level</color>

plants.name: Glow Fire Sun Light Mushroom · Flow Glow Disturb Fly
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"If you look up and see the fireflies swarming, that's me, back by your side." Lumo-shroom never feared death. Born of decayed grass, shining at summer's end, she embraces a final, resolute brilliance to ignite the symphony of rebirth. As night falls, silence spreads, the wheel of yin and yang turns, and the cycle endures forever.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Third form of Lumo-shroom.

<color=blue>PvZ: Symbiosis Collab Plant</color>
<color=black>Sun Output: </color><color=#8B0000>20→35/25s
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> After yet another 150s under Lumos, Lumo-shrooms finally gain the <u>Lumo-burst</u> ability. Each additional Lumos Level accelerates upgrades speed by 100%.
<color=black>•</color> Lumo-burst: When a Lumo-shroom dies, all zombies in a small radius are followed by five Lumo-flies (increased range when grown up).</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Lumo-shroom + Lumos Level</color>

plants.name: Ice Chunck Gift Box
plants.introduce: Among all the gift boxes in the shop, the frozen one is the least loved. Why? Well… it's known for being the victim of cold treatments.
plants.info:
<size=36>Giftbox with a below-average price.

<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Spawns a random frozen base plant.</color>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>50</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>15s</color>

plants.name: Diamond Rock Earth Bean Tough Fruit
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"I was always a weakling growing up, no match for zombies, and nobody wanted to play with me," said Diamond Rock Earth Bean Tough Fruit. "But now, wrapped in diamond, I'm strong—and ready to fight back." He really needs to, though—he's already racked up quite the tab with Gold Goblet Flower.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>A Mine that's as hard and as rare as diamonds.

<color=#4A2900>Usage Condition: Odyssey: Randomized | Easter Egg plant with very rare chance of appearing</color>
<color=black>Toughness: </color><color=#8B0000>32000</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Short plant (evades straight-firing projectiles).
<color=black>•</color> Anti-crush, takes no more than 200 damage per hit.
<color=black>•</color> Produces 500 points on damage phase transitions and on death.</color>

plants.name: Passion Double Sound Artillery
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Passion Double Sound Artillery only fires one shot at a time, so what’s the "double blast"? Ah, it’s the sound of zombie bones cracking.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Floating passionfruit that fires seeds at enemies.

<color=blue>SHUTTLE Collab Plant</color>
<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>40/1.5s</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Hovering plant.
<color=black>•</color> Each hit deals an extra damage equal to 10% of the zombie's max Toughness to the zombie's headgear armor (Type 1).
<color=black>Ultimate: </color>Costs 3000 Coins, Cooldown: 2s; launches a giant passionfruit seed that deals damage to armor equivalent to 20% of current HP.</color>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>100</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>7.5s</color>

plants.name: Frozen Pear
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"I fell from a pear tree, and I've been here a long time," says Frozen Pear. "Too bad there were no genius brains under the tree. But at least I can ponder physics myself." When will he take a break? That depends on when brainless zombies interrupt him.
plants.info:
<size=36>Releases shockwaves when bitten, dealing damage and applying Chill.

<color=blue>SHUTTLE Collab Plant</color>
<color=black>Toughness: </color><color=#8B0000> 4000</color>
<color=#0077b6>Cryo System: </color><color=#8B0000>When bitten, releases a small shockwave every 3s, dealing 20 damage and applying Chill, adding 35 Cryo points.</color>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>150</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>30s</color>

plants.name: Ice Blade Snow Lily
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Though he often shouts "Off with their heads!", Ice Blade Snow Lily isn't a warmonger. As the master of Snowy Mountain martial arts, his ice-morphs-to-blade technique can deter any impure intruder.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Each attack releases 5 ice blades in a fan shape.

<color=blue>SHUTTLE Collab Plant</color>
<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>20×5/1.5s</color>
<color=#0077b6>Cryo System: </color>Blades pierce twice, apply Chill, add 5 Cryo points, and deal ×4 damage to frozen zombies.</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Attacks only when there are zombies in its own row.
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>325</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>7.5s</color>

plants.name: Clear Bud Autumn Chrysanthemum
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Clear Bud Autumn Chrysanthemum loves the snowy plains—every second here is rewarding and joyful. After all, he'd never seen snow before.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Grants nearby plants temporary invincibility.

<color=blue>PvZ: Ultimate Collab Plant</color>
<color=#3D1400>Unlock Conditions: </color><color=red> Complete Adventure Level 45</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
Disappears 2s after planting. Grants all plants in a 3×3 area 6s of immunity to all damage and crushing.</color>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>100</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>50s</color>

plants.name: Grave Tomb Swallow Consumer
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Despite Grave Tomb Swallow Consumer's fearsome appearance, he wants everyone to know that he loves kittens and spends his off hours volunteering at a local zombie rehabilitation center. "It's just the right thing to do," he says.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Plant Grave Tomb Swallow Consumers on graves to remove the graves.

<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Plant Grave Tomb Swallow Consumer on a gravestone to remove it, taking 5s. Drops itself as a seed packet if not planted on a grave.</color>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>75</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>7.5s</color>

plants.name: Black Shiny Rock Big Grain
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>The Black Shiny Rock Big Grain statue in the garden is rumored to be the result of a plants' building game gone rogue. "Who knows," says Black Shiny Rock Big Grain with a chuckle. "I think they saw me as the ultimate 'Build & Guess' challenge long before I arrived. But hey, I don't mind — who wouldn't want more chances to show off their looks?"</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>A sleek, obsidian-like version of Big Grain that periodically transforms into a random Ultimate plant.

<color=#4A2900>Usage Conditions: Replaces Big Grain in Odyssey: Adventure</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Transforms into a random Ultimate plant every 30s (excluding Greed Desire Mushroom)</color>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>500</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>60s</color>

plants.name: Extreme Cold Ice Bean
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>The plants couldn't figure out how such a tiny bean could chill the air so effectively. But Extreme Cold Ice Bean just grinned mischievously. "You know why Cat Tail Grass sneezes when she washes clothes? Because she washes 'cat' clothes." Maybe the real magic was in his sense of humor.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Hey↑ Yeh↓!

<color=#4A2900>Unlock Conditions: Complete Tough Fruit Billiards 1</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Hovering plant.
<color=black>•</color> Strengthens snow pea shooters in a 3×3 surrounding area to launch polar ice peas, increasing the damage and Cryo points inflicted on the zombie by 2 times.</color>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>300</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>30s</color>

plants.name: Fire Red Lily Female King
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>The once-fiery little flower has grown into a master of sunlight production after enduring hardships. Fire Red Lily Female King no longer needs to get hurt to shine. The torch on her head has become her power, blazing like the sun. The shining flames keep passing on, creating one Fire Red Lily after another.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Blasts zombies to dust with solar flares.

<color=blue>PvZ: Big Grain Edition S6 Collab Plant</color>
<color=#4A2900>Usage Conditions: Odyssey Mode</color>
<color=#4A2900>Unlock Conditions: Clear Odyssey Classic</color>
<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>50×8/3s</color>
<color=black>Toughness: </color><color=#8B0000>1000</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Increases damage by 50% for each unique type of Odyssey Plant on the field.
<color=black>•</color> Targets zombies across the screen.
<color=black>•</color> Projectiles return 5 sun on hit and can be upgraded by passing through Super Tier Fire Torch Tree Stumps/Ultra Extreme Fire Burst Squash Torch for x4 damage and the ability to return 15 sun each (with the increased sun value Odyssey Modifier, return 15 and 25 sun instead).<color=#8B0000></color>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>200</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>30s</color>

plants.name: Hamburger King
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Plants often wonder about Hamburger King's origins, especially when they see him stacking other plants on him to enhance his power. In truth, he's just a byproduct of Dave's attempt to make a taco out of plants. "Actual product may vary. Additional fillings are self-provided."</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Assemble the burger as instructed by the labels!

<color=blue>PvZ: Hybrid Edition Collab Plant</color>
<color=#4A2900>Unlock Conditions: Beat Vasebreaker - Chain Reaction</color>
<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>20×2/3s</color>
<color=black>Toughness: </color><color=#8B0000>1000</color>
<color=black>Sun Output: </color><color=#8B0000>25/25s</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000> Tall plant. Fires random projectiles. Upgrades by using Sage Person Palm, West Melon Thrower, and Ice Melon to reach the highest level. Go to the next page for info on the upgrades.<color=black>

|</color> Sage Person Palm Upgrade <color=black>|</color> Level: 0 → 1
<color=black>•</color> Toughness: 1000 → 2000
<color=black>•</color> Attack Interval: 3 → 2.5
<color=black>•</color> Num. of Shots: 2 → 4
<color=black>•</color> New Projectiles: Spike, Butter, Squash Melon

<color=black>|</color> West Melon Thrower Upgrade <color=black>|</color> Level: 1 → 2
<color=black>•</color> Toughness: 2000 → 3000
<color=black>•</color> Attack Damage: 30 → 40
<color=black>•</color> Num. of Shots: 4 → 6
<color=black>•</color> New Projectiles: Hot Melon, Melon

<color=black>|</color> Ice Melon Upgrade <color=black>|</color> Level: 2 → 3
<color=black>•</color> Toughness: 3000 → 4000
<color=black>•</color> Attack Interval: 2.5 → 2
<color=black>•</color> Num. of Shots: 6 → 8
<color=black>•</color> New Projectiles: Ice Melon, Cold Ice Shooter, Snow Spore, Doomspike<color=#8B0000></color>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>150</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>30s</color>

plants.name: Fuse Together Ocean Taro
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"I'm a grand magician to rival the great Model Imitater," proclaimed Fuse Together Ocean Taro. However, only his good friend, Model Imitater, knows the truth: Fuse Together Ocean Taro and Dr. Edgar used to study life sciences in the same class.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Catalyst for Titan fusions.

<color=#4A2900>Unlock Conditions: Beat Fuse Together Ocean Taro Challenge</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>A fusion catalyst for Titan plants. Place it on the middle plant to start the fusion. Titan fusions are indicated with "∞". Titan plants are immune to Blizzards.<color=#8B0000></color>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>50</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>30s</color>

plants.name: Model Imitater
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"I remember the Rigid Corpse Wars back in 76", says Model Imitater in a raspy, old-man's voice. "Back then, we didn't have all these fancy peashooters and jalapenos. All we had was guts. Guts and a spoon."</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Model Imitater can make copies of your plants!

<color=#4A2900>Unlock Conditions: Beat Odyssey Gacha - Model Imitater</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Plant Model Imitater on a plant or fusion to receive a free seed packet of that plant.
<color=black>•</color> Cannot imitate pots, pads, pumpkins or drones. 
<color=black>•</color> Failure returns an Model Imitater seed packet.</color>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>100</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>5 mins</color>

plants.name: Cat Melon
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"The grace of a cat and the clumsiness of a squash make a unique kind of beauty," Cat Melon murmurs. "Once I master both, I'll stir things up on this lawn." She adjusts her hat and locks eyes on an unsuspecting zombie nearby.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Super Neko, Cat Melon!

<color=#4A2900>Unlock Conditions: Beat Squash Melon Showdown</color>
<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>1800 (Squash Melon)</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Drops itself if it doesn't hit anything. For each zombie crushed, a Squash Melon seed packet is dropped, up to a maximum of 3. Crushing 3 or more zombies will drop an additional Fire Burst Squash Melon seed packet. This does not trigger in water lanes.</color>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>125</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>30s</color>

plants.name: Sword Sage Foreign Peach
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"I have fought across 3,000 miles of battlefield, my blade halting millions in their tracks." The Sword Sage Foreign Peach, a lover of fine drinks and poetry, takes great pride in his impeccable cosplaying skills. With a sly smile, he adds, "My sword never bleeds, for it is merely a decoration."</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>A guest from afar, I think you will want to become friends.

<color=#4A2900>Unlock Conditions: Beat The Gods 1</color>
<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>(200×5+30×20)/6s</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Each attack unleashes 5 swords that pierce infinitely, dealing 200 damage each. Additionally, it inflicts 30 damage to all zombies within the surrounding area, repeating this 20 times.</color></size>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>325</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>30s</color></size>

plants.name: Rigid Corpse Blind Box
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Introducing the Rigid Corpse Blind Box—the zombies' entry point into the world of finance. As a high-risk financial product, it comes equipped with a reminder: "Investing carries inherent risks; proceed with caution. Sponsored by Dr. Yan Wang." This vital information is cleverly tucked inside the box lid.</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Earn 100 Sun, but spawns a Yellow Gold Matryoshka Rigid Corpse.

<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Grants 100 sun, but spawns a Yellow Gold Blind Box Rigid Corpse. (Regular/Conehead/Iron Buckethead Duck Save Life Circle Rigid Corpse in water lanes)</color></size>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>-100</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>50s</color></size>

plants.name: Giant Size Sun Light Tough Fruit
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"Sun Light Emperor Fruit asked me to do him a favor for 500 Sun, so I came. How difficult can it be? It can't be rushing into a pile of zombies... right...? It's not, right!?"</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Crushes zombies by rolling on them like a bowling ball.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>600/0.02s</color></size>
plants.cost: <size=36>Spawned when Sun Light Emperor Fruit is clicked when there's sufficient Sun.

plants.name: Magic Way Cat Girl
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"Cats are usually afraid of water, but I'm a magical cat girl, so I'm not afraid," she said enthusiastically, and the other cats looked at her with admiration. Actually, she absolutely loves water, and she said this to make herself stand out.</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>The Magic Way Cat Girl will automatically attack all zombies that appear on the map. Prioritizes flying zombies.

<color=blue>P"Girls"vZ Collab Plant</color>
<color=#4A2900>Unlock Conditions: Beat Attack On Gargantuar!</color>
<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>40×2/1.5s</color></size>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>125</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>50s</color></size>

plants.name: Big Grain
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Big Grain calls itself a generalist, but other plants think of it as just a bit player — "After all, other plants always do the work, don't they?"</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Big Grain regularly transforms into other plants.

<color=#4A2900>Unlock Conditions: Beat I, Rigid Corpse (Sandbox Included) via Normal Mode</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Transforms into a random non-Odyssey fusion (or upgrade) plant every 30s. Transformation stops after further manual fusion.</color></size>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>50</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>30s</color></size>

plants.name: Fire Red Lily
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>It is said that small flowers like her would never dream of producing Sun — the Fire Red Lily was originally going to apply for the position of Towards Sun Flower, but she realized that her light would only shine when she is being destroyed. Everyone says that the flame at the top of her head is the brightest, but she knows that her fiery eyes of determination are the source of her strength.</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Fire Red Lily can absorb projectiles and produce Sun.

<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Absorbs at most 6 projectiles, each producing 25 Sun. Produces a Fertilizer upon death.</color></size>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>125</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>50s</color></size>

plants.name: Giant Size Tough Fruit
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"I am invincible on the battlefield, and my partners can't take their eyes off my majestic figure!" the Giant Size Tough Fruit boasted. "One year after getting kicked out of the Tough Fruit Tumbler Club, I finally found a job that truly suits me."</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Giant Size Tough Fruit rolls forward, knocking down all zombies in its path.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>600/0.02s</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Rolls down the lane, killing all the zombies on its path.</color></size>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Tough Fruit + Tough Fruit</color>

plants.name: Surprise Happy Gift Box
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>No one quite understands why the Surprise Happy Gift Box fights alongside the plants against the zombies. He doesn't look like a plant at all, yet he seems to know every plant's abilities inside and out. "Actually, I'm just a college student who loves video games," he admits with a grin. "The professor mentioned extra credit, so here I am."</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>The Surprise Happy Gift Box can be planted to produce random plants or fusions.

<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>When placed on soil or on a Sleep Lily, produces a random plant. Placing it on top of an eligible plant can produce a random fusion.</color></size>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>100</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>15s</color></size>

plants.name: Ultra Extreme Sword Sage Foreign Peach
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"The flowers in the hall intoxicate three thousand guests, and the sword frosts fourteen states." "I won't board the ship when the emperor calls me, and I say I am a fairy in wine." Ultra Extreme Sword Sage Foreign Peach likes to recite poems and practice his signature sword skills when he is drunk. The plants marveled at his power to defeat the zombies, but he said that he was aiming his flying sword at the bright moon in the sky, but when he put the sword away, it would fall back to the earth. </color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Ultra Extreme Sword Sage Foreign Peach sweeps through enemies and summons ten giant swords descending from the sky.

<color=blue>Unsealed form of Sword Sage Foreign Peach</color>
<color=#3D1400>Usage Conditions:
<color=black>•</color> Abyss Challenge (Level 11-30), upgraded from Sword Sage Foreign Peach after beating Level 10. You can only have 1 Ultra Extreme Sword Sage Foreign Peach on the lawn.
<color=black>•</color> Unlockable in Purgatory Cursed Mode</color>
<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>(1800×2+1800×10)/6s</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>When attacking, deals 1800 damage to zombies within a 3×3 area twice, then summons 10 giant swords that seek out enemies across the entire field, each dealing 1800 area damage upon landing.</color>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>750</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>30s</color>

plants.name: Ultra Extreme Speed Shoot Cherry Peach Shooter
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Other plants can't stop envying his explosive firepower. He just shrugs it off: "I'm no genius here. It's all just hard work and sweat." Of course, he is referring to how he secretly stole everyone else's equipment and strapped it onto himself.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Stronger than the strongest damage-dealer.

<color=blue>Unsealed form of Ultra Extreme Cherry Peach Shooter</color>
<color=#4A2900>Usage Condition: Harvest Mode | Legendary Odyssey Plant</color>
<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>300×6/0.5s</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000> Shoots explosive cherries at a horrifying rate after charging up for a 1.5s.</color>

plants.name: Ultra Extreme Blood Moon God Roll Heart Vegetable
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>When the sky is painted in crimson light, war follows in her wake. Where peace cannot be found, she descends. She is the herald of bloodspill, the bringer of death: the Goddess of the Blood Moon.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Born of the night, calling to fight. Under her blood moon, scarlet tides of zombies rise again and again.

<color=blue>Unsealed form of Ultra Extreme Moon Bright God Roll Heart Vegetable</color>
<color=#4A2900>Usage Condition: Harvest Mode | Legendary Odyssey Plant; Easter Egg Plant</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Has a 2% chance to replace Ultra Extreme Moon Bright God Roll Heart Vegetable when fusing in Night settings (Night, Fog, Night Roof). Ultra Extreme Blood Moon God Roll Heart Vegetable retains all basic traits of Ultra Extreme Moon Bright God Roll Heart Vegetable.
<color=black>•</color> While Ultra Extreme Blood Moon God Roll Heart Vegetable is on the field, Mini Moons from Ultra Extreme Moon Bright God Roll Heart Vegetables become Mini Bloodmoons. These no longer provide Healing, but instead summon buffed Hypnotized Rigid Corpses every 10s. The first summon has a 3s cooldown.
<color=black>•</color> Each Ultra Extreme Blood Moon God Roll Heart Vegetable provides a +50% Toughness and Damage buff to the summoned Rigid Corpses.
<color=black>•</color> The number and position of summons depend on "barren tiles," defined as tiles without an Arteris or Ultra Extreme Moon Bright God Roll Heart Vegetable on them or in their 3×3 area.</color>

plants.name: Ultra Extreme Model Imitater Big Grain
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Ultra Extreme Model Imitater Big Grain is a master of imitation. Ever since joining a imitation show, even the originals he mimicked could only take second place.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Regularly transforms into nearby offensive or defensive plants, growing stronger through mimicry.

<color=#4A2900>Usage Condition: Harvest Mode</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Every 30s, transforms into a random plant within a 3×3 area.
<color=black>•</color> Gains +100% attack and +50% max HP on the first successful transformation; each subsequent transformation grants an additional +30% attack and +20% max HP.
<color=black>•</color> Can transform into: non-flying plants whose damage is not 0 or toughness exceeds 300, excluding pumpkins, lily pads, pots, and other barley-type plants (and their transformations).</color>

plants.name: Ultra Extreme Hot Sun Towards Sun Flower
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Legend says she once served the Sun itself, carrying its fiercest light within her radiant bloom. When danger draws near, Ultra Extreme Hot Sun Towards Sun Flower turns that blessing into judgment - every beam of sunlight she fires is a decree of holy fire.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Forges her produced sun and nearby projectiles into solar judgments that rain down from above, causing area explosions.

<color=blue>Unsealed form of Ultra Extreme Towards Sun Flower</color>
<color=#4A2900>Usage Condition: Harvest Mode | Legendary Odyssey Plant</color>
<color=black>Sun Output: </color><color=#8B0000>50/3s</color>
<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>1800 (Cremator)</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> When producing sun, if zombies are present, converts the sun into a solar judgment and fires it at the nearest zombie.
<color=black>•</color> When firing a solar judgment, absorbs plant-side projectiles within a 1.5-tile radius, gaining +2% damage per projectile absorbed.
<color=black>•</color> Solar Judgments: Falls from the sky, dealing AoE damage (1.5-tile radius) and dropping 50 sun; can target flying zombies.
<color=black>Ultimate: </color>Costs 16000 Coins; fires 1 solar judgment at every zombie present.</color>

plants.name: Without Fantasy Mushroom
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Without Fantasy Mushroom hails from the deepest, most crowded abyss and commands the most elusive dreamscapes. In her, the balance of yin and yang is perfectly realized, and even powerful monsters have been tamed by her hand. In private, she is also a veteran collector of zombie figurines.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Summons a hynotized Mini-boss to fight for you.

<color=#4A2900>Usage Condition: Harvest Mode | Legendary Odyssey Plant</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> 1.5s after appearance, randomly summons a hypnotized Mini-boss zombie that fights for you from the following. Summoned hypnotized Mini-boss cannot be pulled back by Charm Confuse 3 Leaf Grasss or captured by Ultra Extreme Charm Confuse South Melon.
<color=black>•</color> Abyssal Gargantuar: Also provides a 90% incoming damage reduction for all plants while present.
<color=black>•</color> Giga Football Striker: Also heals all plants by HP equal to 0.2× their max HP every 3s while present.
<color=black>•</color> Professor Cherryz: Immobile. Shoots one explosive cherry every 0.1s. Loses his newspaper on appearance and converts up to 30 non-miniboss, non-gargantuar zombies on the field from both sides into enraged hypnotized Cherry Peach Machine Gun Shooter Read Newspaper Rigid Corpses.</color>

plants.name: Charm Emperor Mushroom
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Charm Emperor Mushroom holds great affection for her zombie subordinates. "They contributed significantly to my rise as an Empress," she expressed. Unfortunately, these powerful zombies instill fear in other plants, something Charm Emperor Mushroom is completely unaware of.</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Summons zombies that will fight for you.

<color=#4A2900>Usage Condition: Odyssey Mode</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Hypnotizes biters up to 5 times.
<color=black>•</color> Every 30 seconds, randomly selects 1 of the following zombies to summon:
Olive Tall Tough Fruit Rigid Corpse | Enraged Cherry Peach Shooter Read Newspaper Rigid Corpse | Pea Commando Football | Snow Sled Ice Vehicle Rigid Corpse | Studyr Rigid Corpse | Machine Instrument Sea Pig Rigid Corpse.
<color=black>•</color> Instantly summons a zombie when an Charm Queen Mushroom dies within a 3×3 range.
<color=black>Odyssey Modifiers:
</color><color=#8B0000>1. Summon Interval → 10s | Charm Queen Mushroom Subspecies Unlocked
2. Can now summon corresponding Ultimate Rigid Corpses</color></size>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Charm Confuse Mushroom + Charm Confuse Mushroom</color>

plants.name: Ultra Extreme Cherry Peach Shooter
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"I'm not sure why the Machine Gun Shooter has so many fans. My brother and I can fire four bullets too," Ultra Extreme Cherry Peach Shooter remarked enviously. However, the truth is that everyone appreciates Machine Gun Shooter for his kindness and outstanding culinary talents.</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Ultra Extreme Cherry Peach Shooter is the strongest damage-dealer on land.

<color=#4A2900>Usage Condition: Odyssey Mode | Unlockable</color>
<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>300×4/1.5s</color>
<color=black>Main Odyssey Modifiers:
</color><color=#8B0000>1. Explosive cherries from plants ×3 damage
2. Attack Interval halved</color>

<color=black>Ultra Extreme Fire Burst Squash Torch Synergy Modifier:</color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> You must have unlocked all Ultra Extreme Cherry Peach Shooter and Ultra Extreme Fire Burst Squash Torch's modifiers before this synergy becomes available in the shop for 15k.
<color=black>•</color> Ultra Extreme Cherry Peach Shooter now fires equal amounts of projectiles across three lanes when attacking, with the upper and lower projectiles following sine and cosine trajectories. Ultra Extreme Fire Burst Squash Torch unleashes Fire Burst Spicy Pepper effect across the entire field if there's no available space to spawn a Fire Burst Squash Melon.</color></size>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Cherry Peach Machine Gun Shooter + Super Tier Cherry Peach Shooter</color>

plants.name: Ultra Extreme Fire Burst Squash Torch
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Ultra Extreme Fire Burst Squash Torch has a high opinion of himself, but other plants regard him with disdain, particularly those in the pea family. "He's too picky! He possesses such strong power, yet it only benefits cherries," they say. This infuriates Ultra Extreme Fire Burst Squash Torch. "I'm just allergic to peas…"</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Spawns a Fire Burst Squash Melon after igniting a number of projectiles.

<color=#4A2900>Usage Condition: Odyssey Mode | Unlockable</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Inherits Super Tier Fire Torch Tree Stump's traits, but does not burn zombies.
<color=black>•</color> Explosive cherries that pass through it become bouncing spicy squashes that explode after every bounce.
<color=black>•</color> For every 21 projectiles ignited, spawns a Fire Burst Squash Melon in front.
<color=black>Main Odyssey Modifiers:
</color><color=#8B0000>1. Projectiles required to spawn a Spicy plant are halved
2. Ignites the lane immediately when spawning a Spicy plant is not possible</color>

<color=black>Ultra Extreme Cherry Peach Shooter Synergy Modifier:</color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> You must have unlocked all Ultra Extreme Cherry Peach Shooter and Ultra Extreme Fire Burst Squash Torch's modifiers before this synergy becomes available in the shop for 15k.
<color=black>•</color>Ultra Extreme Cherry Peach Shooter now fires equal amounts of projectiles across three lanes when attacking, with the upper and lower projectiles following sine and cosine trajectories. Ultra Extreme Fire Burst Squash Torch unleashes Fire Burst Spicy Pepper effect across the entire field if there's no available space to spawn a Fire Burst Squash Melon.</color></size>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Super Tier Fire Torch Tree Stump + Fire Burst Squash Melon</color>

plants.name: Ultra Extreme Cherry Peach Battle God
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Ultra Extreme Cherry Peach Battle God proudly says, "Many envy my dinosaur-like hairstyle." As a public figure, he's mindful of his image: "Spitting cherries and eating zombies is gross, but it's my duty."</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Ultra Extreme Cherry Peach Battle God is the ultimate defensive plant on land.

<color=#4A2900><color=#4A2900>Usage Condition: Odyssey Mode | Unlockable</color></color>
<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>1000/1.75s</color>
<color=black>Toughness: </color><color=#8B0000>8000</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Tall plant. Repels vehicles and takes at most 1/3 maximum Toughness per hit.
<color=black>•</color> Heals when hit by explosive cherries (same effect as Cherry Peach Tough Fruit). Excess HP drains at 800 per second, capped at 4× Toughness.
<color=black>•</color> Every 40s, can devour a zombie (deals 1m damage except against mini-bosses), restoring HP to 4x Toughness and gaining 1 Devour stack.
<color=black>•</color> Bites deal AoE damage and bonus damage equal to zombie's max HP×(3% + 0.2%× Devour stacks), healing for the same amount. After biting, spits out an explosive cherry.
