<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Hides himself by default. Using a Sea Mushroom seed packet on him makes him attack for 10s, after which he cowers again. Is short when hiding.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Little Courage Mushroom > Sea Mushroom</color>

plants.name: “ Tall ” Tough Fruit Shooter
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"Three ordinary nuts together are smarter than Zhuge Liang!" “ Tall ” Tough Fruit Shooter joined forces, becoming inseparable companions and unleashing unimaginable potential. These three have a knack for physics, and apparently fluid dynamics is their strongsuit.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Three small-nuts stacked on top of each other.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> (12-8k HP) 80, pierces twice;
<color=black>•</color> (8-4k HP) 60, pierces 4 times;
<color=black>•</color> (4k-0 HP) 40, pierces 6 times</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Tall plant. Has 12k HP in total. Each Small-nut has 4k HP. Losing one Small-nut permanently reduces max HP by 4k.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>3 Line Shooter + Tough Fruit</color>

plants.name: Search Treasure Earth Bean Thunder
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Beneath the ground lie treasures… and zombies? "So my treasure-hunting garden is actually a graveyard?!" Search Treasure Earth Bean Thunder only wants to dig for treasures, but Dave buried zombies to make the hunt more exciting, which complicates things.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Always busy digging for treasures.

<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Short plant. Digs up a treasure every 30s: 1% chance for a random zombie, 10% for a random seed packet, otherwise produces 25 Sun and 100 Coins.
<color=black>•</color> On Beach maps, Search Treasure Earth Bean Thunders placed on Treasure Spots gain 50% faster production; seed packet chance rises to 33% while zombie chance drops to 0%.
<color=black>•</color> Disappears after finding 3 seed packets.
<color=black>•</color> On death or disappearance, triggers a Earth Bean Thunder explosion with 1800 damage.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Gold Goblet Flower + Earth Bean Thunder</color>

plants.name: Cold Ice Spike Pig Mushroom
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"Hey, stay away from me! Let me be alone quietly, or I'll stab you with ice spikes!" Cold Ice Spike Pig Mushroom inflates to scare approaching enemies. Watching the ice spikes protruding out towards the zombies, the other plants can't help but wonder: who's really approaching whom?</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Inflates after consuming Sea Mushrooms, emitting cold air around himself.

<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Short plant (evades straight-firing projectiles). Loses the ability to attack. Using a Sea Mushroom seed packet on him makes him swell up and freeze all zombies in a 3×3 area, applying chill and dealing 20 damage.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Cold Ice Mushroom > Sea Mushroom</color>

plants.name: Pea Big Spray Mushroom
plants.introduce:
<align=left><size=36><color=black>"To pea or not to pea?" for Pea Big Spray Mushroom, that is the question. From a young age he rejected shooting peas. He stated he would rather help other plants. when asked where his desire to help others came from, he said, "Road Lantern Flower has been a huge role model for me growing up."

-Written by Berfy Boy</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Long-range sprayer; his fume can also give projectiles extra momentum.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>20/1.5s</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Has full-lane range. While emitting fumes, boosts straight-shooting projectiles ahead, increasing their speed and +1 damage every 0.02s. The boost is stackable.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Pea Shooter + Big Spray Mushroom</color>

plants.name: Tough Fruit South Melon
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>For the Tough Fruit South Melon, all that matters is creating a good internal environment for the smaller Nuts to grow. He ignores the outside noise and even zombie bites. The only thing he cares about is other plants crowding his internal space, a space he reserves specifically for the Mini-nuts.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Continuously grows helpful Mini-nuts inside.

<color=black>Toughness: </color><color=#8B0000>8000</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Spawns a Mini-nut every 50s. When the Mini-nut is fully grown, it is consumed to heal a nearby damaged South Melon-type plant for up to 1000 HP or to produce a Tough Fruit seed packet if no such plant exists. A single Tough Fruit South Melon can produce at most 3 Tough Fruit seed packets.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Tough Fruit > South Melon</color>

plants.name: Giant Mushroom
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Can mushrooms grow on mushrooms? According to Professor Cherryz, Giant Mushrooms are rare large composite colonies, but they refuse to admit they are a collection of many individuals. To prove this, they try with all their might to eject extra spores.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Dominates an area with fumes and spores.

<color=black>Toughness: </color><color=#8B0000>1000</color>
<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>(20+20×8)×4/1.9s</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Emits an extra long-ranged spore in 8 directions each attack.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Little Spray Mushroom ∞ [Worry Gloom Mushroom] ∞ Little Spray Mushroom < Fuse Together Ocean Taro (Place On Middle)</color>

plants.name: Protect Guard Umbrella Little Mushroom
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Like other Umbrellas, Protect Guard Umbrella Little Mushrooms have great ambitions and are willing to sacrifice themselves to protect the world. Though not tall enough to protect other plants, they have found something else to protect, the land they live on and love so deeply.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Uses their small body to desperately fend off enemies.

<color=black>Toughness: </color><color=#8B0000>300</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Short plant (evades straight-firing projectiles).
<color=black>•</color> Repels the first zombie on contact, then vanishes.
<color=black>•</color> Against shrunken zombies, repels them and loses 50 HP instead.
<color=black>•</color> Spawns a new Protect Guard Umbrella Little Mushroom in his tile every 30s.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Leaf Protect Guard Umbrella > Little Spray Mushroom</color>

plants.name: Sea Mushroom Group
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Reproduction and proliferation are life's instincts. Under certain conditions, Sea Mushroom Groups grow together, resisting waves, and swim in schools to confuse predators. In recent years, technology enabling compact breeding has finally been developed and immediately applied in aquariums.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Highly reproductive; an important part of the ocean fungal ecosystem.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>20×2 (later ×3)/1.5s</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Does not return Sun on fusion.
<color=black>•</color> Short plant (evades straight-firing projectiles).
<color=black>•</color> Starts out as a school of 2.
<color=black>•</color> Grows into a school of 3 after 15s.
<color=black>•</color> Afterwards, feeds the plant directly in front with a Sea Mushroom every 15s. If unsuccessful, drops a Sea Mushroom seed packet.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Little Spray Mushroom > Sea Mushroom</color>

plants.name: Destroy Eradicate Sea Whale Mushroom
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>They say this giant used to swim in the open seas of Sharktooth Ridge, the terror of all fish. But one fateful mishap with a certain Shrink Shroom bubble somehow left him tiny. His body's shrunken, but not his appetite, nor his penchant for explosive enthusiasm when a Sea Mushroom drifts too close.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>After consuming Sea Mushrooms, spits small Doom-bombs forward.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>600</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Short plant (evades straight-firing projectiles).
<color=black>•</color> Launches a Doom spore that deals 600 damage in a moderate radius after consuming a Sea Mushroom. Feeding has no CD, but each shot takes 1.5s to fire and only a max of 2 shots can register at the same time.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Destroy Eradicate Mushroom > Sea Mushroom</color>

plants.name: Fire Torch Towards Sun Flower
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Fire Torch Towards Sun Flower is always cheerful, even able to smile through her doctoral dissertation. "Become the sunshine you have always been looking for." she wrote in the acknowledgments at the end of her paper.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Melts nearby projectiles into Sun!

<color=black>Sun Output: </color><color=#8B0000>25/25s</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Each time she produces Sun, converts all plant (and hypnotized zombie) projectiles in a 1.5 tile radius into Sun at a rate of 5 Sun per projectile.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Towards Sun Flower + Fire Torch Tree Stump</color>

plants.name: Water Mother Charm Confuse Mushroom
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"Am I a Jellyfish? Or a Mushroom? Guess wrong, and you'll have to work for me." The Water Mother Charm Confuse Mushroom loves playing these games with brainless zombies and has never lost. The closest to the right answer was Professor Cherryz, who answered "I know Box Jellies are not true Jellies… Are you one of those?"</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Summons grunt zombies to fight for you after consuming a Sea Mushroom.

<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Summons a hypnotized Duck Save Life Circle Rigid Corpse (browncoat) that fights for you after consuming a Sea Mushroom.
<color=black>•</color> When bitten, hypnotizes the attacker, then disappears.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Charm Confuse Mushroom > Sea Mushroom</color>

plants.name: Olive Machine Gun Shooter
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"Foul play! You're out!" Olive Machine Gun Shooter is the strictest and most hot-tempered referee on the field, upholding football rules. From start to finish, his mouth never stops, constantly trying to eject players who don't understand the game.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Occasionally fires red shots that lure Olive Ball Rigid Corpses away.

<color=black>Toughness: </color><color=#8B0000>3000</color>
<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>80×4/2s</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Metal plant. Counts as an Advanced Fusion.
<color=black>•</color> Projectiles knock back zombies and instantly break Type 2 armor (before damage reduction).
<color=black>•</color> Every 12th shot is a Football Pea that causes hit Red-colored Olive Ball Rigid Corpses to run away from your house.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Olive Hat > Machine Gun Shooter</color>

plants.name: Sun Light Foreign Peach
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"I'm Sun Light Foreign Peach, not Sunny Five-fingers! Don't mix us up!" Sun Light Foreign Peach always tries to explain that he's different from Sunny Five-fingers, but his plant friends don't care. The only supporters he has are the zombies, since they think fingers are definitely more annoying to gnaw on than fruits.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Uses Sun to shoot high-damage stars.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>100×5</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Click on a Sun Light Foreign Peach to spend 25 Sun and make it shoot 5 100-damage Stars in 5 directions.
<color=black>•</color> Uses Sun and shoots automatically every 0.35s if placed in a Foreign Peach South Melon.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Towards Sun Flower + Foreign Peach</color>

plants.name: Sun Light Spicy Pepper
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Why do so many plants feel distant from her? She can't figure it out: "I can both defend you and bring you sunshine!" She believes she's brighter and sunnier than Fire Burst Spicy Pepper, but other plants think she's more destructive.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Burns other plants to enhance her power.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>1800 (Cremator)</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Returns 25 Sun for every zombie hit.
<color=black>•</color> Also deals 1800 damage to all plants in her lane. Each affected plant drops 50 Sun and boosts Sun Light Spicy Pepper's damage by 500. If a plant would be destroyed, it has a 1/3 chance to survive with 1 HP remaining.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Towards Sun Flower + Fire Burst Spicy Pepper</color>

plants.name: Flow Clear Tough Fruit
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"Streetlights sometimes malfunction, and that’s normal. This is when my role as a backup streetlight becomes important, though everyone seems worried about me," said Flow Clear Tough Fruit. He didn't know that others worry not about his brightness, but that he might break the streetlight frame.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Tough Fruit with Road Lantern Flower properties; heals himself with Lumos level.

<color=black>Toughness: </color><color=#8B0000>4000</color>
<color=#99582a>Lumos System: </color><color=#8B0000>Provides 1 Lumos Level in a 3×3 area. Restores 150 Toughness per Lumos Level every 15s.</color></size>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Tough Fruit + Road Lantern Flower</color>

plants.name: Little Courage Tough Fruit
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Little Courage Tough Fruit was originally shy like other Little Courage Mushrooms, until seeing distant relatives with "Mushroom Power" on TV — unafraid of zombies. He decided to learn from them, nicknamed himself "Mushroom Lee," and trained day and night to face zombies without fear.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Very slim defender, but will fight back when cornered.

<color=black>Toughness: </color><color=#8B0000>4000</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000> After cowering for 10s, immediately stands up and knocks back nearby zombies, dealing 20 damage to the zombie's body, its Type 1 armor and Type 2 armor simultaneously. For the next 5s, he will not hide himself again. While cowering, takes 50% reduced damage.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Tough Fruit + Little Courage Mushroom</color>

plants.name: Tough Fruit Flower Pot
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Everyone speculated about Tough Fruit Flower Pot's appearance: some said he was a broken nut, others that he was a damaged daruma doll. Whatever others guessed, Tough Fruit Flower Pot didn't answer. Those boring past events were buried by lava. "At least now, I am a Pot," he said.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Heat-insulated defensive Flower Pot.

<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Reduces incoming damage to the plant on top by 20%.
<color=black>•</color> Immune to Lava tile damage and protects the plant on top.
<color=black>•</color> Lava tiles deal 50 damage per second to plants and zombies on them, prioritizing Flower Pots, dealing 10 damage to them instead.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Tough Fruit > Flower Pot</color>

plants.name: Tough Fruit Big Mushroom
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Tough Fruit Big Mushroom trained a batch of Tough Fruit Little Mushrooms but didn't teach them combat. When asked, "How do we defend against zombies?" he replied: "With a hard shell and do-or-die determination!" Then he launched them at the zombies. "This is a technique I learned from baseball," he announced proudly.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Picks up nearby Tough Fruit Little Mushrooms to attack.

<color=black>Toughness: </color><color=#8B0000>4000</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000> Picks up Tough Fruit Little Mushrooms within a 3×3 area and bats them forward as projectiles. Projectile damage equals the Tough Fruit Little Mushroom's HP and ignores Type 2 armor. If the projectile hits a zombie and deals enough damage to kill it, the shot pierces through, with its damage reduced by the amount dealt.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Tough Fruit + Big Spray Mushroom</color>

plants.name: Water Grass Tough Fruit
plants.introduce:
<align=left><size=36><color=black>Everyone says Water Grass Tough Fruit is very reclusive, with such tangled problems that it is difficult to unravel to reach such a hard heart. But who can deny his desire for solitude? No one, except Cat Tail Grass, who sometimes mistakes him for a ball of string to play with.

-Written by Ultra Badlander</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Applies Tangle value as retaliation.

<color=black>Toughness: </color><color=#8B0000>4000</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000> When bitten, inflicts 1 Tangle value on the attacker. When crushed, loses 50 HP every 0.02s and inflicts 1 Tangle value each time.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Tough Fruit > Tangle Surround Water Grass</color>

plants.name: Magnet Force West Melon Thrower
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Magnet Force West Melon Thrower used to work at the Electromagnetics Research Institute and once proposed a conjectured model of magnetic poles, suggesting that magnetic poles are distributed like watermelon seeds embedded in a watermelon. However, his theory was ignored, because he was only the institute's watermelon supplier.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Stepping stone for other Metal Melon fusions, also deals heavy damage to helmeted zombies.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>120/3s</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000> Deals 2× damage to zombies with Type 1 Armor. Can fuse with Iron Bucket and Chrono Core.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>West Melon Thrower + Magnet Force Mushroom</color>

plants.name: Iron Bucket West Melon Thrower
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Covering his body in iron saved the Iron Bucket West Melon Thrower from ending up at the fruit stall, but he still faces many troubles, like spending heavily on rust-proofing and constantly answering other curious plants: "Are your leaves also made of iron? Can iron leaves be used as fruit knives?"</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Lumps heavy balls of pure iron at the zombies, dishing out heavy damage.

<color=black>Toughness: </color><color=#8B0000>2000</color>
<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>200/3s</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Metal plant. Deals 2× damage to zombies with Type 1 Armor.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Iron Bucket > Magnet Force West Melon Thrower</color>

plants.name: Super Time Space West Melon Thrower
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"I'm just standing on the shoulders of giants!" Super Time Space West Melon Thrower humbly says. "I never had the power to reverse time; I only spread temporal chaos."</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Spreads Chronoshift effect via splash damage.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>80/3s</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Splash damage is equal to main damage and has no falloff.
<color=black>•</color> If a zombie directly hit or in splash radius has Chronoshift effect, up to 3 splash targets (6 if buffed by West Melon Flower Pot) will also be afflicted with the effect.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Chrono Core > Magnet Force West Melon Thrower</color>

plants.name: Destroy Eradicate Spicy Pepper
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"I'm about to explode! Everyone in front of me better watch out, plant or zombie alike! This is mutual destruction, assured!" said Destroy Eradicate Spicy Pepper. His slightly trembling body indicates a massive energy build-up, or maybe it's just from standing on tiptoes with an unstable stance.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Also burns your plants to trigger chain explosions.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>1800 (Cremator)</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Does not explode automatically; click manually to trigger a Fire Burst Spicy Pepper blast and a Destroy Eradicate Mushroom explosion.
<color=black>•</color> In every other tile in the same lane that has a plant, releases a Destroy Eradicate Mushroom explosion without leaving craters and destroys all plants in that tile; the destroyed plants have a 25% chance to drop as a Seed packet.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Fire Burst Spicy Pepper + Destroy Eradicate Mushroom</color>

plants.name: Pea South Melon
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Heard that some zombies brought a "tank"? Then show them the living machine-gun fortress — Pea South Melon! As for ammo… well, the designer forgot to include any! But no problem! Let the peashooters inside "share" some ammo!</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Copies the behavior of the Pea-shooting plant inside it.

<color=black>Toughness: </color><color=#8B0000>4000</color>
<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>?</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>If a Pea-family plant is inside, it copies the attack of that plant, matching that plant's projectile count, damage, and type and shoots at 50% the attack rate, with a min. attack CD of 3s and a max. of 6s. These shots are considered to be fired by the internal plant.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Pea Shooter > South Melon</color>

plants.name: Fire Spicy Pea Shooter
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>This Pea Shooter loves roasted peas so much that even on the battlefield, he snacks between shots. "Ahh! My peas!" - looks like he accidentally spat out his snack again.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Fires Fire Spicy Pea Shooters that inflict Enflamed.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>30/1.5s</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000> Attacks inflict Enflamed debuff.
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Pea Shooter + Fire Burst Spicy Pepper</color>

plants.name: Fire Spicy Double Shot Shooter
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"Double firepower, double thrill, double fun! Compared to Fire Spicy Pea Shooter, I like eating hotpot twice in a row!" He doesn't realize that Super Tier Fire Torch Tree Stump now has double the workload.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Fires Fire Spicy Pea Shooters in pairs.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>30×2/1.5s</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Attacks inflict Enflamed debuff.
<color=black>•</color> Tap to auto-fuse with a free Pea Shooter 15s after planting.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Double Shot Shooter + Fire Burst Spicy Pepper</color>

plants.name: Fire Spicy Crack Pod Shooter
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"Damn it! Where's our support? Machine gunner on the left, move right!" Hot-tempered and easily enraged, this shooter often yells at teammates, though luckily, he hasn't started firing at them yet.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Fires Fire Spicy Pea Shooters in triple bursts.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>30×3/1.5s</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Attacks inflict Enflamed debuff.
<color=black>•</color> Tap to auto-fuse with a free Pea Shooter 15s after planting.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Crack Pod Shooter + Fire Burst Spicy Pepper</color>

plants.name: Fire Spicy Machine Gun Shooter
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Tired of treating endless burn victims, this pea doctor now takes his proud invention of pyrotherapy to cure the problem at its source.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Fires 4 Fire Spicy Pea Shooters at once.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>30×4/1.5s</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000> Attacks inflict Enflamed debuff.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Machine Gun Shooter + Fire Burst Spicy Pepper</color>

plants.name: Big Garlic South Melon
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>After countless revisions, Big Garlic South Melon is finally combat-ready. Compared to regular Big Garlic, it fixes issues like weak defense, space efficiency and inability to protect plants from projectiles. As for that smell… well, time to test everyone's endurance.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>South Melon with Big Garlic traits.

<color=black>Toughness: </color><color=#8B0000>4000</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000> Rigid Corpses that bite him will be poisoned for 10s, forced to change lanes and accumulate 1 Poison point.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Big Garlic > South Melon</color>

plants.name: Sun Light South Melon
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Recently obsessed with camping, Sun Light South Melon beams: "Camping's the best! I get to shield everyone and be their little sun!" Rain or zombies, her teammates love having her around.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Generates Sun while reinforcing plants.

<color=black>Toughness: </color><color=#8B0000>4000 + ?</color>
<color=black>Sun Output: </color><color=#8B0000>25/25s</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000> Gives 50 HP as Shield to all plants in her tile every time she produces Sun. The total Shield value has no upper cap.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Towards Sun Flower > South Melon</color>

plants.name: Sun Light Cold Ice Mushroom
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>As rare as a polar sunrise, this frosty innovator spends all his time perfecting his craft of dessert-making. His signature sundae is the Sunny Orange Delight, loved by plants and zombies alike (even if it drains the last of their warmth).</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Gradually melts, releasing cold and sunlight in stages.

<color=black>Sun Output: </color><color=#8B0000>25/25s</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> At each sun production, makes all frozen zombies drop 15 sun.
<color=black>•</color> For the first two sun production cycles, triggers Cold Ice Mushroom effects that inflict chill but don't freeze. On the third cycle, triggers full Cold Ice Mushroom effect, then disappears.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Towards Sun Flower + Cold Ice Mushroom</color>

plants.name: Sun Light Charm Confuse Mushroom
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Every year, she joins the garden darting contest - hitting zombies flying, snorkling, hypnotized, or even hypnotized with her sun-shaped darts. Despite her flawless precision, she never wins… the judges can never find her.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Produces Sun; upon being bitten, releases a Solar Comet that ricochets among zombies.

<color=black>Sun Output: </color><color=#8B0000>25/25s</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> When bitten, hypnotizes the attacker and summons a Solar Comet.
<color=black>•</color> The Solar Comet seeks out zombies (prioritizing hypnotized ones) and bounces between them, up to 10 times. When it hits a hypnotized zombie, it deals 200 damage and drops 25 Sun. When it hits a non-hypnotized zombie, it deals 100 damage and drops 15 Sun.
<color=black>•</color> After the Comet completes its bounces or disappears, it drops an additional 25 Sun.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Towards Sun Flower + Charm Confuse Mushroom</color>

plants.name: Big Mouth South Melon
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Big Mouth South Melon opened a gym for Big Mouth Flowers, meant to help them digest better, burn fat, and stay in shape. It became wildly popular, but he's not happy. "They're not training, they're eating! My gym’s become a buffet!" he vents.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Provides digestive aids for Big Mouth Flower-type plants inside his shell to speed up digestion.

<color=black>Toughness: </color><color=#8B0000>4000</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Periodically uses a reserve Digestive Sac to help the Big Mouth Flower plant in the same tile to instantly finish digesting (or to instantly recharge the Devour abilities of Super Tier Big Mouth Flowers and its fusions).
<color=black>•</color> Generates 1 Digestive Sac every 40s, up to a maximum of 3 at the same time. First Digestive Sac takes 7.5s to generate.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Big Mouth Flower > South Melon</color>

plants.name: Garlic Poison 3 Line Shooter
plants.introduce:
<align=left><size=36><color=black>"Ugh, your taste stinks! I hate you two!" cries the third head. Rigid Corpses think he's crying from his fellows' smell, but actually, it's just because of their terrible taste in music. "We don't even have noses," the first head explains.

-Written by silver fool</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Shoots Poison peas; makes poisoned zombies release toxic clouds on hit.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>30×3/1.5s</color>
<color=#5E1675>Poison System: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Projectiles add 1 Poison value to zombies on hit.
<color=black>•</color> If the zombie hit is already in a Poisoned state, creates a Toxic Cloud at the zombie's feet that lasts for 6s, applying 1 Poison value and dealing damage equal to 20× the total projectiles fired in one volley by all Poison-system straight-shooting plants in this lane per second to all zombies caught within. There can be up to 1 active Toxic Cloud per lane.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>3 Line Shooter + Big Garlic</color>

plants.name: Disease Poison Snipe Strike Shooter
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>She doesn't actually carry viruses, but everyone avoids her - that strong garlic smell is enough. "The smell actually prevents viruses," she insists. The zombies don't buy it.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Snipes targets to spread an Infectious Cloud that multiplies like a virus.

<color=#4A2900>Usage Condition: Odyssey Mode</color>
<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>500/3s</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000> Creates an Infectious Cloud at the target's location. Headshots create a Virulent Cloud instead.</color>
<color=#5E1675>Infectious Clouds: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Moves randomly across the lawn.
<color=black>•</color> Lasts 15s. Every second, inflicts Big Garlic Butter and 1 Big Garlic Point to zombies inside (Virulent Clouds have doubled radius and tick rate).
<color=black>•</color> When inflicting Big Garlic Points, deal (20×Big Garlic Points) damage. If the target is immune to poison, deals 300 damage instead.
<color=black>•</color> Targets that have lost their heads (HP below threshold) die instantly, spawning a new Infectious Cloud at their position.
<color=black>•</color> If 30 Infectious Clouds already exist, new ones won't spawn and instead expand the nearest Cloud's radius and increase its damage by 10%.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Snipe Strike Shooter + Big Garlic</color>

plants.name: Cherry Peach 3 Line Shooter
plants.introduce: "Delicious cherries!" "Fresh cherries!" "Cheap cherries!" Cherry Peach 3 Line Shooter's three mouths shout tirelessly, yet their cherry business never seems to take off. Those who have tried them say these cherries are just too sticky.
plants.info:
<size=36>Fires half-ripe cherries across three lanes. They stick to zombies and mature after a short delay, then explode.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>20×3/1.5s</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Projectiles attach to targets and explode after 6s with a 1-tile splash radius. Each zombie can only have one half-ripe cherry attached.
<color=black>•</color> While attached, half-ripe cherries absorb 25% of all cherry-type projectile and cherry explosion damage the zombie takes, adding that to its final explosion damage.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Cherry Peach Explode Bullet + 3 Line Shooter</color>

plants.name: Cherry Peach Super Tier Machine Gun Shooter
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>At the 5th Annual Garden Quickdraw Championship, Cherry Peach Super Tier Machine Gun Shooter shared his secret to victory: "I use specially crafted cherry rounds. Their small size means I can store plenty, and the stickiness helps them cluster together for rapid bursts. Of course, keeping the barrel well-lubricated is absolutely essential."</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Shoots half-ripe cherries at zombies, with a small chance of shooting a massive barrage!

<color=#4A2900>Usage Condition: Odyssey Mode</color>
<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>20×6/1.5s</color>
<color=black>Ultimate: </color><color=#8B0000>20×3/0.02s</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>2% chance to shoot a barrage of half-ripe cherries each attack, lasting 5s. These explode instantly and attach a half-ripe cherry to all zombies in the damage radius.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Cherry Peach Explode Bullet + Super Tier Machine Gun Shooter</color>

plants.name: Destroy Eradicate Cherry Peach
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"You can't host a party in the garden without a permit." Destroy Eradicate Cherry Peach casually tells the unregistered zombies off. "The first time's just a warning. If they still don't leave after that, I'll take action. Well, some can't even handle the warning."</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Unleashes a Cherry Peach Explode Bullet blast, then uses the resulting ash to trigger an even stronger Destroy Eradicate Mushroom explosion.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>1800 (Cremator)</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Triggers a Cherry Peach Explode Bullet explosion on fusion, and explodes like a Destroy Eradicate Mushroom after 1.5s.
<color=black>•</color> The Destroy Eradicate Mushroom blast deals 1800 base Cremator damage, increasing by a factor of (# of zombies hit by the initial Cherry bomb explosion) × 1/6. (For reference, if one zombie is hit by the initial cherry explosion, the cremator damage will be 7/6 times the original damage).</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Cherry Peach Explode Bullet + Destroy Eradicate Mushroom</color>

plants.name: Cherry Peach Charm Confuse Mushroom
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Cherry Peach Charm Confuse Mushroom was nearly expelled from the Zombuddies Club. The zombies she hypnotized always turned furious and destructive, which the club considered contrary to its peaceful values. She defended herself with a single sentence: "I'm merely helping my zombie friends vent their frustration toward Dr. Edgar."</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>The catalyst for making zombies more explosive.

<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Upon being bitten, turns the zombie into a Hypnotized Super Tier Cherry Peach Shooter Rigid Corpse and disappears.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Cherry Peach Explode Bullet + Charm Confuse Mushroom</color>

plants.name: Sea Mushroom South Melon
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Once a star performer at the World Marine Life Festival and the pride of the Sea Mushroom colony, Sea Mushroom South Melon can no longer float on water. Everything changed after that dreadful Halloween party. He wore a pumpkin head for the perfect performance, only to find he could never take it off again.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Regenerates itself using Sea Mushroom seed packets.

<color=black>Toughness: </color><color=#8B0000>4000</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Land plant. Can be fed Sea Mushroom seed packets to heal by 1/3 max HP.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Sea Mushroom > South Melon</color>

plants.name: Charm Confuse Big Mouth Flower
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Charm Confuse Big Mouth Flower looks down on his fellow Big Mouth Flowers. He thinks they're rude, picky eaters, and lazy freeloaders who produce nothing for the garden, unlike him, with his dazzling, radiant skin.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Devours hypnotized zombies and converts them into Charm Confuse Mushrooms.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>Insta-kill (Devour)</color>
<color=black>Digestion Time: </color><color=#8B0000>40s</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Also targets Hypnotized zombies and drops a Charm Confuse Mushroom seed packet on a successful devour of a Hypnotized zombie, then digests for 10s.
<color=black>•</color> When bitten, hypnotizes the attacker, then reverts to a Big Mouth Flower.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Big Mouth Flower + Charm Confuse Mushroom</color>

plants.name: Charm Confuse Spicy Pepper
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"I'm actually very harmless," said Charm Confuse Spicy Pepper, adjusting his hat. "I help zombies find their hidden fragility, guiding them to drop their pretense and become open, honest friends." As for the blazing flames? "That's just the hypnotium doing its magic. It doesn't really hurt the fragile zombies."</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Explodes with fiery charm, causing zombies to switch sides.

<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Hypnotizes all zombies with less than 900 HP in its lane. Deals 1800 Cremator damage to all other zombies in its lane.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Charm Confuse Mushroom + Fire Burst Spicy Pepper</color>

plants.name: Yellow Oil Ground Spike
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Unlike other Ground Spikes, Yellow Oil Ground Spike is exceptionally gentle. It has no sharp spikes, dislikes violent sports, and just wants zombies to try a slice of its homemade butter cake. Unfortunately, with his poor eyesight, he always offers the butter to the wrong spot.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Sticky, buttery slow trap that doesn't deal damage.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>0</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Slows down all non-flying zombies that walk over it and Butters them for 1s every 3s.
<color=black>•</color> No longer pops tires and is ignored by vehicles.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Ground Spike + Jade Rice Thrower</color>

plants.name: Cold Ice Road Lantern Flower
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Winter is the season for meditation. The cold sharpens the mind. With gnawing winds and silent nights, such a perfect time for reflection should not be wasted. For days on end, the night-watching Cold Ice Road Lantern Flower has been pondering what he should be thinking about. Oh wait, that's it! Thinking about what to think about!</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Freezes zombies with a cold aura and enables lasers to gain chilling effects.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>20/1s</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Inherits Road Lantern Flower traits.
<color=black>•</color> Once per second, deals 20 damage and applies Chill to zombies in within a 1.1-tile radius, and prolongs the Freeze duration of the zombies already frozen by 1s (cannot be reapplied to the same zombie).
<color=black>•</color> Anti-ground Lasers from Flow Light Sage Person Palm/Flow Light Big Lantern Mushroom turn cyan and gain the ability to apply Chill after passing through.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Cold Ice Mushroom + Road Lantern Flower</color>

plants.name: Sea Mushroom Ground Thunder
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>After lying in wait for a long time, Sea Mushroom Ground Thunder has drawn sufficient strength from sunlight and moisture, gaining power comparable to a Cherry Peach Explode Bullet. "Spudow!" he shouted, but the zombies were only surprised.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Can grow twice.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>900 (Initial)</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Short plant (evades straight-firing projectiles).
<color=black>•</color> Starts in an armed state. Grows 30s after appearance, doubling its damage.
<color=black>•</color> After another 30s, grows again and increases explosion radius to 1.5 tiles across lanes.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Sea Mushroom > Earth Bean Thunder</color>

plants.name: Sea Mushroom Water Thunder
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>The moment he enters the pool, Sea Mushroom Water Thunder absorbs water like a little water baby, convinced this will greatly amplify his explosive power. While this does turn out to be true, he still cannot understand how it all works, just as he cannot understand why the pool is never completely filled with dead zombies either.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Aquatic and can grow twice.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>900 (Initial)</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Short plant (evades straight-firing projectiles).
<color=black>•</color> Starts in an armed state. Grows 30s after appearance, doubling its damage.
<color=black>•</color> After another 30s, grows again and increases explosion radius to 1.5 tiles across lanes.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Earth Bean Thunder > Sea Mushroom</color>

plants.name: Destroy Eradicate Road Lantern Flower
plants.introduce:
<align=left><size=36><color=black>Destroy Eradicate Road Lantern Flower doesn't consume darkness to create light. Their light is fueled by their contempt for the Rigid Corpses, and a couple grams of plutonium. "My light burns with rage. Pray you don't find it pointed at you."

-Written by Goober</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>His UV light is radioactive and grants lasers explosive effects.

<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Inherits Road Lantern Flower traits.
<color=black>•</color> Every 3s, supplies Remaining Ember Tree Stumps in a 3×3 area with 3 Irradiation value.
<color=black>•</color> Anti-ground Lasers from Flow Light Sage Person Palm/Flow Light Big Lantern Mushroom turn purple and gain the ability to trigger a 1-tile explosion dealing 10 damage on hit after passing through.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Destroy Eradicate Mushroom + Road Lantern Flower</color>

plants.name: Roll Heart Vegetable Mortar Strike Artillery
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>With a thunderous boom, bright-green shells soar into the sky. "A farewell gift," says Roll Heart Vegetable Mortar Strike Artillery with a nod. "May their last meal be filling, even if it is not brains. Everyone gets a serving."</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Bombards a wide area ahead with cabbages.

<color=black>Toughness: </color><color=#8B0000>1000</color>
<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>40/0.75s</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> When attacking, lobs one cabbage at each zombie in the forward area of his lane and the 2 adjacent lanes.
<color=black>•</color> Roll Heart Vegetable Protect Guard Umbrella or Green Treasure Rock Umbrella in front in of his lane can also intercept cabbages that would have been thrown to adjacent lanes and boost them.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Roll Heart Vegetable Thrower ∞ [Roll Heart Vegetable Thrower] ∞ Roll Heart Vegetable Thrower < Fuse Together Ocean Taro (Place on Middle)</color>

plants.name: West Melon Squash Squash Thrower
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Squash Melon is heavy, but too soft. Melons are heavy too, but too fragile. Now those problems are solved. Or possibly combined.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Projectiles crush zombies like a Squash Melon, then shatter like a Melon, dealing splash damage.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>120 (Squash Melon)/3s</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Projectiles deal area crush damage on landing, then break shortly after, dealing splash damage with fall-off similar to West Melon Thrower.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Squash Melon + West Melon Thrower</color>

plants.name: Cherry Peach Squash Melon
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Even among Squash Melones, Cherry Peach Squash Melon is famously short-tempered and quick to explode. "Try carrying something that heavy on your back," he grumbles. Other Squash Melones are less concerned about the temper, and more curious how he could jump so high and so far with the thing on his back.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>A very explosive Squash Melon.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>1800 (Squash Melon) + 1800 (3×3, Cremator)</color>
<color=black>Detection Range: </color><color=#8B0000>2 tiles to the left; 3 tiles to the right</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Upon landing, triggers a Cherry Peach Explode Bullet explosion.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Squash Melon + Cherry Peach Explode Bullet</color>

plants.name: Destroy Eradicate Squash Melon
plants.introduce:
<align=left><size=36><color=black>Destroy Eradicate Squash Melon rarely speaks to anyone on the lawn. It silently moves from place to place, leaving craters behind. Rumor has it that it was originally supposed to be deployed under "dire circumstances" but somehow escaped containment.

- Edited by cassidy</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>An extremely explosive Squash Melon, and prone to causing collateral damage.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>1800 (Squash Melon) + 1800 (5×5, Cremator)</color>
<color=black>Detection Range: </color><color=#8B0000>2 tiles to the left; 3 tiles to the right</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Upon landing, triggers a Destroy Eradicate Mushroom explosion that leaves a crater on the landing spot; destroying all plants on that tile.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Squash Melon + Destroy Eradicate Mushroom</color>

plants.name: Yellow Oil Sticky Sticky Mushroom
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"Come on, have a taste of my fresh butter!" Yellow Oil Sticky Sticky Mushroom says proudly, showing off his new recipe. "If you don't like it, just shake your head," he adds. Everyone seems to be diggin' it, or at least they are completely motionless.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Sprays butter from the top of its cap onto nearby zombies.

<color=black>Attack Range: </color><color=#8B0000>An area within a 2.2-tile radius</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Does not actively target zombies. When damaged or manually tapped and if there are zombies within attack range, it performs an attack.
<color=black>•</color> When attacking, loses 30 max HP (and current HP), throwing one pat of Butter at every zombie in range, dealing 40 damage and immobilizing them for 4s</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Big Spray Mushroom + Jade Rice Thrower</color>

plants.name: Special Type 3 Line Shooter
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"You two have already taken turns for three whole minutes, now it's my turn!" The three heads of the Special Type 3 Line Shooter have been competing for shared items since childhood, from marbles, to comic books, to a limited-edition commando badge carved from peas.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Can switch between three attack modes, allowing flexible allocation of firepower.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>20×6(or n)/1.5s</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Has three attack modes that can be switched by tapping on it; targeting behavior changes accordingly:
<color=black>•</color> 1. Standard Mode: Fires 2 peas each into his own lane and the 2 adjacent lanes;
<color=black>•</color> 2. Spread Mode: Fires 1 peas each into every lane (if the map has fewer than 6 lanes, the damage from missing shots is evenly redistributed among the remaining shots);
<color=black>•</color> 3. Focus Mode: Fires 6 peas each into his own lane.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Pea Shooter + 3 Line Shooter</color>

plants.name: Sun Light 3 Leaf Grass
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Sun Light 3 Leaf Grass always wears a gentle smile, often saying, "Clear skies and a spring breeze filled with sunshine - how pleasant!" Sadly, zombies don't understand this beauty. What she blows out isn't a soft spring wind, it's pure solar wind.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Capable of blowing out a large number of Sun shots.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>10×72</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Spins its leaves and fires two spirals totaling 72 Sun Shots. Each shot drops 5 Sun upon hitting a target and can be buffed by Super Tier Fire Torch Tree Stump to deal 40 damage and drop 15 Sun instead.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Towards Sun Flower + 3 Leaf Grass</color>

plants.name: Little Mushroom South Melon
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>The three Little Spray Mushroom South Melon brothers are well-loved, well-behaved kids: quiet, orderly, and never quarrelsome. Their only hobby is trying to piece together the pumpkin puzzle on top of their heads, though unfortunately, they have never quite succeeded.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>The more Little Spray Mushrooms in the Little Mushroom South Melon, the stronger the protection!

<color=black>Toughness: </color><color=#8B0000>4000</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Short plant (evades straight-firing projectiles).
<color=black>•</color> Restores 50 HP every 3s. Starts out at 1/4 max HP.
<color=black>•</color> At 1/3 max HP or more, gains a 50% incoming damage reduction.
<color=black>•</color> At 2/3 max HP or more, absorbs damage for the plant inside.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Little Spray Mushroom > South Melon</color>

plants.name: Big Spray Mushroom South Melon
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"I can attack in both forward and backward directions," says Big Spray Mushroom South Melon confidently. "I'm a bidirectional Big Spray Mushroom." However, he doesn't appear to have noticed that he's facing a different direction from everyone else.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Can attack like a Big Spray Mushroom… though the direction seems a little off.

<color=black>Toughness: </color><color=#8B0000>4000</color>
<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>20×3/1.5s (Piercing)</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000> Targets from his own tile, up to 3 tiles both above and below</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Big Spray Mushroom > South Melon</color>

plants.name: Sun Light Squash Melon
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Sun Light Squash Melon's favorite thing to do is gently pat the petals on his head with his leaf, then boast proudly to other Squash Melones: "This is what Sun-powered shampoo does. Just look how smooth my hair is." Luckily for him, the other Squash Melones are all very serious and never tease him, so he still doesn't know he's sporting a classic receding hairline.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Can consume Sun to remain on the lawn after squashing.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>1800 (Squash Melon)</color>
<color=black>Detection Range: </color><color=#8B0000>0.5 tiles to the left; 1 tile to the right</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Upon landing or dying, consumes 50 + 25 * (Number of previous revivals) Sun to remain on the tile of the landing spot/revive. If this fails, 50 Sun is refunded.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Towards Sun Flower + Squash Melon</color>

plants.name: West Melon South Melon
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"Ha ha! Guess where my eyebrows are!" Melon South Melon laughs at the audience. A famous rooftop magician, he excels at all kinds of tricks, including making half of his eyes disappear. Of course, his proudest illusion is the Great Melon Transformation. To this day, no one has ever seen how he hides a watermelon inside a West Melon Thrower's bucket.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Throws bonus watermelons when protecting a catapult-type plant.

<color=black>Toughness: </color><color=#8B0000>4000</color>
<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>80/?s</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Each time the catapult plant inside attacks, it simultaneously launches an additional watermelon.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>West Melon Thrower > South Melon</color>

plants.name: Earth Bean Squash Melon Chunck Stem
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>You've seen Squash Melones growing on vines, but have you ever seen one sprouting from the ground itself? Don't be fooled, as Earth Bean Squash Melon Chunck Stem is simply mashed potatoes shaped like a Squash Melon. But don't you dare laugh at how short he is. If he hears you, he'll make you even shorter than he is.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Produces Earth Bean Squash Melones that can squash zombies.

<color=black>Detection Range: </color><color=#8B0000>0.5 tiles to the left; 1 tile to the right</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Short plant (evades straight-firing projectiles).
<color=black>•</color> Starts out armed by default. Generates a Earth Bean Squash Melon on the spot when a zombie enters his targeting range, then burrows underground for 15s to arm again.
<color=black>•</color> Earth Bean Squash Melon has the same effect and damage as Squash Melon, but with a larger targeting range.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Earth Bean Thunder + Squash Melon</color>

plants.name: Earth Bean Squash Melon
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Don't let his small size fool you - his ambitions are anything but small. "I won't live under the Masher's protection forever. I'm jumping out to find my own life." With that, he leaps forward and crushes the zombie in his way. Maybe one day, he too will find a pit of his own, just like the Masher did.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Similar to Squash Melon, but much easier to trigger.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>1800 (Squash Melon)</color>
<color=black>Detection Range: </color><color=#8B0000>1.5 tiles to the left; 2.5 tiles to the right</color>

plants.name: Destroy Eradicate Big Garlic
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>At one Rigid Corpse Big Garlic-Eating Championship, Destroy Eradicate Big Garlic was served as the final course. The result: three of the ten contestants withdrew, five were rushed to the hospital, and the remaining two exploded on the spot. With no remaining competitors, Destroy Eradicate Big Garlic himself was declared the champion of that year's event.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Causes the zombies that bit it to explode upon death.

<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Short plant (evades straight-firing projectiles).
<color=black>•</color> Inflicts Irradiated debuff on zombies that bite it and curses them. Cursed zombies explode on death in a 1.5-tile radius for 300 damage.
<color=black>•</color> Inflicts Poison debuff and 1 Poison point on zombies that bite it.
<color=black>•</color> When Irradiated debuff and Poison debuff are active simultaneously on a zombie, the Poison duration becomes infinite.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Destroy Eradicate Mushroom + Big Garlic</color>

plants.name: Foreign Peach Big Mushroom
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>When Foreign Peach Big Mushroom's star-shaped cap hits a zombie, it releases star projectiles, greatly improving projectile efficiency. "Actually, if the backward-fired star were optimized as well, the efficiency could be even higher." One of his comrades commented while dodging the returning projectile.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Fires piercing star caps that split into five stars on contact.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>0 + 20×5×(0-3)/1.5s</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>The Star cap itself deals no damage and can pierce twice. Each time it contacts a zombie, it releases 1 star projectile in each of 5 directions, each dealing 20 damage.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Big Spray Mushroom + Foreign Peach</color>

plants.name: Little Courage Flower Pot
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>At school, Scaredy Flower Pot always curls up and avoids interacting with others. Because the mushrooms he grows are fewer than those of his classmates, he believes he is inferior. What his fear prevents him from noticing is that his classmates are actually envious - the mushrooms he grows are the most delicious of all.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Give boosts based on the value of the plant placed on top.

<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Grants the plant placed on top an attack damage bonus equal to [0.04 × (that plant's Sun cost)]%, up to 20%.
<color=black>•</color> If the plant on top is a Scaredy-type mushroom, the attack bonus becomes 2×(base bonus + 10%), up to 60%.
<color=black>•</color> Becomes frightened when zombies are present within a 3×3 area and stops providing attack bonus while frightened. After leaving the frightened state, the attack bonus gradually recovers over 3s.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Little Courage Mushroom > Flower Pot</color>

plants.name: Flow Light Big Lantern Mushroom
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"Every plant I see is wearing sunglasses," Flow Light Big Lantern Mushroom grumbles. "It's ridiculous, so pretentious!" In the end, Flow Light Big Lantern Mushroom chalks it up to some inexplicable trend, while the other plants agree that staring directly at an ultra-high-power laser emitter is obviously bad for your eyesight…</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Fires a piercing laser ray. Damage falls off with distance and increases with Lumos.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>10/1.5s</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Fires across the entire lane with unlimited pierce.
<color=black>•</color> Each Lumos level increases damage by 0.5×.
<color=black>•</color> Damage increases by 1.6×(5 - column distance to the target). At Lumos level 3 or higher, this instead becomes 0.8×(10 - column distance).</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Big Spray Mushroom + Road Lantern Flower</color>

plants.name: Roll Heart Vegetable Float Ship
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>After years of mindlessly throwing cabbages, seeing the world at that height really makes you think. What if those cabbages had a life? Hopes and dreams? Families? Children? All of those questions run through Roll Heart Vegetable Float Ship's leafy mind.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Provides close-range air support by dropping cabbages.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>40×3/3s</color>
<color=black>Attack Range: </color><color=#8B0000>Own tile and 2 adjacent tiles in the same lane</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Flying plant.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>3 Leaf Grass + Roll Heart Vegetable Thrower</color>

plants.name: Jade Rice Float Ship
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Jade Rice Float Ship signed up for the Garden Variety Show, hoping to play a UFO on stage, but it never made the cut. He just kept dropping corn and butter everywhere, drawing fierce opposition from the ground crew, and even causing the director who supported him to take a nasty fall.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Provides close-range air support by dropping corn kernels or butter.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>20×3/3s</color>
<color=black>Attack Range: </color><color=#8B0000>Own tile and 2 adjacent tiles in the same lane</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Flying plant. Has a 25% chance to drop butter, dealing 40 damage.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>3 Leaf Grass + Jade Rice Thrower</color>

plants.name: West Melon Float Ship
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Other melons call him lazy, but West Melon Float Ship insists he's efficient. "Just let gravity do the work! Throwing things is exhausting!" He seems to completely ignore the fact that this also shortens his range.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Provides close-range air support by dropping melons.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>80×3/3s</color>
<color=black>Attack Range: </color><color=#8B0000>Own tile and 2 adjacent tiles in the same lane</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Flying plant.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>3 Leaf Grass + West Melon Thrower</color>

plants.name: Earth Bean Big Spray Mushroom
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>After accepting a brand partnership offer from Earth Bean Thunder, Earth Bean Big Spray Mushroom quit his bakery job. "Joining the explosive-making industry was the best decision I've ever made," says Earth Bean Big Spray Mushroom. "Spraying starch straight from your mouth is way more efficient than fermenting bread!"</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>The fume he sprays carries a strong potato scent, sometimes even explosive!

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>30/1.5s</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Every 10th attack, triggers a Earth Bean Thunder explosion that deals 300 damage at the position of each zombie hit.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Earth Bean Thunder + Big Spray Mushroom</color>

plants.name: Jade Rice Squash Squash Thrower
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Jade Rice Squash Squash Thrower was never good at biology. He's always believed that the idea of "apical dominance" is wrong, but he's learned to keep that opinion to himself, not because he's embarrassed about his grades, but because he doesn’t want anyone to realize he's a corn with an underdeveloped tip.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Thrown projectiles slam zombies like a Squash Melon, with a chance to butter the ones that get crushed.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>60/3s (Squash Melon)</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Projectiles deal area squash damage when they land. Has a 25% chance to throw a Butter Squash Melon, dealing 60 damage and applying 4s of Butter stun to up to 3 damaged targets.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Squash Melon + Jade Rice Thrower</color>

plants.name: Destroy Eradicate Earth Bean Thunder
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>As the head chef of a French fry restaurant, Destroy Eradicate Earth Bean Thunder runs a tight kitchen and is known for his strict, no-nonsense management. But behind his successful spud career lies a personal struggle: his explosive temper means he can no longer make delicious fries.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Shatters in a devastating explosion, scattering five Earth Bean Thunders that instantly arm on landing.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>1800 (Cremator)</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Short plant. When it explodes, it also triggers a Destroy Eradicate Mushroom effect, then throws potato chunks onto 5 empty tiles. Each chunk creates a fully armed Earth Bean Thunder as soon as it lands.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Earth Bean Thunder + Destroy Eradicate Mushroom</color>

plants.name: Foreign Peach Charm Confuse Mushroom
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Foreign Peach Charm Confuse Mushroom worries that the loose tuft of fuzz on top of his cap makes him look silly, though most zombies would never understand why that bothers him.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Rigid Corpses that bite him become hypnotized, turning into moving star launchers.

<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>When bitten, hypnotizes the zombie and disappears, causing it to fire stars in a spiral: 60 volleys (300 total star projectiles).</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Charm Confuse Mushroom + Foreign Peach</color>

plants.name: Pea Squash Melon
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"Should I jump up and smash zombies, or should I just be a proper shooter?" That question haunted Pea Squash Melon throughout his entire adolescence. In the end, he chose the more traditional Squash Melon lifestyle, but every time he hits the ground, those little green bumps on his body reflexively fire peas anyway.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Bursts into a storm of peas when it slams down.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>1800 (Squash Melon)</color>
<color=black>Detection Range: </color><color=#8B0000>0.5 tiles to the left; 1 tile to the right</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>When it lands, releases 3 rings of pea projectiles (108 peas total).</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Pea Shooter + Squash Melon</color>

plants.name: Silver Cold Ice Mushroom
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Ruthless. Cold-blooded. Those are the labels people stick on Silver Cold Ice Mushroom, even other Cold Ice Mushrooms think he's too icy. Silver Cold Ice Mushroom doesn't care. His success comes from being cold-hearted; anyone who isn't ruthless enough simply can't survive in the cold-chain logistics business.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>An Cold Ice Mushroom coated in silver frost.

<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Freezes for 1 second longer than a normal Cold Ice Mushroom.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Cold Ice Mushroom + Gold Goblet Flower</color>

plants.name: Gold Cold Ice Mushroom
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>A monopolist of the cold-chain logistics industry, Gold Cold Ice Mushroom can sway the temperature of the market with just a light sprinkle of coins. After years in the business, his wealth and fame are beyond counting, and his life is complete… except for the fact that he still can't cure his facial paralysis.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>A stable, gold-plated Cold Ice Mushroom that brings extreme cold through money.

<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Triggers an Cold Ice Mushroom effect once on appearance (does not disappear afterwards).</color>
<color=black>Ultimate: </color><color=#8B0000>Costs 3000 Coins, Cooldown: 2s; repeats the following effects 4 times on targets within a 2.2-tile radius:
-Deals 200 damage, applies Chill and 25 Cryo point, and reduces the target's Cryo cap by 100.
-Fires an icicle at each target dealing 20 damage. Icicles deal 2× damage to Chilled zombies and 4× damage to frozen zombies.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Upgrade Cost: </color><color=#8B0000>3000 Coins</color>

plants.name: Roll Heart Vegetable Ping Pong Mushroom
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Table tennis is a shared passion of both plants and zombies, and Rebound Shroom is a household name in the zombie world, having defeated countless graveyard champions. By reporting opponents for the foul of returning the ball with their heads, he has claimed gold medals in over a dozen Plant-Rigid Corpse Table Tennis Championships, while his not-too-bright opponents have never noticed that he commits the very same foul himself.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Treats projectiles as table tennis balls, batting them back and forth continuously toward zombies' heads.

<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> When a lobbed projectile in the same lane hits a zombie in front, it will rebound toward Roll Heart Vegetable Ping Pong Mushroom and then continue bouncing back and forth between the two.
<color=black>•</color> Only one projectile can be bounced at a time; the bounce height gradually decreases until it hits the ground.
<color=black>•</color> Does not bounce projectiles that can already bounce on their own.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Big Spray Mushroom + Roll Heart Vegetable Thrower</color>

plants.name: Little Squash Melon
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>They're just three kids pestering you for candy. The only unusual thing is that if you refuse their request, you might feel a bit of "pressure."</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Energetic little squashes that hop around and smash enemies.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>300 (Squash Melon)</color>
<color=black>Detection Range: </color><color=#8B0000>1 tile to the left and right</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Short plant. Can be planted 3 times in one tile.
<color=black>•</color> Smashes once every 1s and does not disappear after smashing; when not targeting, jumps back to its original tile.
<color=black>•</color> Vulnerable to zombie attack for 0.6s after each smash.
<color=black>•</color> Immune to crush. When crushed, takes 500 damage every 0.02s. When smashed, takes damage equal to the corresponding damage a hypnotized zombie would take when smashed.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Squash Melon > Little Spray Mushroom</color>

plants.name: Destroy Eradicate South Melon
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Destroy Eradicate South Melon is always advertising his services as being great for healthier-looking skin, but plant inspectors often complain that his treatments are dangerously radioactive.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Channels the power of Destroy Eradicate Mushroom into the plants inside.

<color=black>Toughness: </color><color=#8B0000>4000</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Gains 1% charge every 0.5s; enters the field with a starting charge between 80%-95% randomly.
<color=black>•</color> When charge meter is full (100%), can consume all charge to transform one plant on his tile into a fusion of that plant and Destroy Eradicate Mushroom (the resulting plant must not be an Odyssey fusion).</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Destroy Eradicate Mushroom > South Melon</color>

plants.name: Ground Spike Flower Pot
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Every plant that's spent time with Ground Spike Flower Pot complains that he's far too enthusiastic and has no sense of personal boundaries. Only Black Steel Ground Spike Tough Fruit doesn't mind, and the reason is simple: Ground Spike Flower Pot is the only one willing to help de-rust Black Steel Ground Spike Tough Fruit's razor-sharp shell.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Has Ground Spike traits and enhances the defenses of Ground Spike Tough Fruit plants.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>20/1s</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Short plant. Inherits traits from Ground Spike (ignored by biting zombies and pops tires).
<color=black>•</color> Grants 50% damage reduction to Ground Spike Tough Fruit and Black Steel Ground Spike Tough Fruit on him.
<color=black>•</color> Becomes anti-crush if the plant above is an Black Steel Ground Spike Tough Fruit.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Ground Spike > Flower Pot</color>

plants.name: Flow Light Pea Shooter
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Lumos Pea started out as a kid who loved shining laser pointers at people. Later, Flow Light Sage Glow Umbrella taught him how to use that trick on zombies instead.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Fires high-frequency targeted lasers; absorbs Lumos energy to increase power.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>10/0.2s</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Gains Lumos energy equal to Lumos level every 0.5s, up to 1000. Each laser beam consumes (1 + current energy × 10%) energy when attacking or transferring.
<color=black>•</color> Can lock onto a Flow Light Middle Continue Shooter in front in the same lane (not directly adjacent). Transfers energy every 0.1s, restoring energy equal to 10% of its attack. Each point of energy spent doubles the amount transferred.
<color=black>•</color> Consumes energy every 0.2s to attack, increasing damage by the amount of energy spent.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Pea Shooter + Road Lantern Flower</color>

plants.name: 3 Leaf Squash Melon
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>By harnessing gravitational potential energy, floating Squash Melones can unleash devastating slams with ease: making the exaggerated wind-up before attacking seem unnecessary. "Not unnecessary at all," explains 3 Leaf Squash Melon. "I'm shooing away the ladybugs resting on my leaves, so I don't hit them by accident."</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Repeatedly slams down on zombies beneath him.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>100/2s (Squash Melon)</color>
<color=black>Detection Range: </color><color=#8B0000>Current tile</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000> Flying plant. When a zombie is in range, smashes the zombie below repeatedly.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Squash Melon + 3 Leaf Grass</color>

plants.name: Cold Ice Roll Heart Vegetable Thrower
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"You have no idea how terrifying that place was," Cold Ice Roll Heart Vegetable Thrower says, still shaken. "A land of extreme cold, where countless plants were frozen forever. If I hadn't risked my life to open the door back outside, we vegetables would've all died in there." Oh, he's talking about his adventure inside Dave's refrigerator again.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Lobs frozen cabbages and attacks faster when hitting frozen enemies.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>40/3s</color>
<color=#0077b6>Cryo System: </color><color=#8B0000>Attacks inflict Chill, add 10 Cryo points and deal ×4 damage to frozen zombies.</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>On hitting a Frozen zombie, the projectile can bounce once, and Cold Ice Roll Heart Vegetable Thrower's attack speed is greatly increased for 3s. This effect can only trigger again 1.5s after it ends.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Cold Ice Mushroom + Roll Heart Vegetable Thrower</color>

plants.name: Cold Ice Jade Rice Thrower
plants.introduce: <color=#3D1400>To freeze the perfect butter, Cold Ice Jade Rice Thrower made a special trip to Siberia. Now, its butter is as smooth as marble; heavy, hard, beautiful, and makes a satisfying thud when smashed on a zombie's head.'</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Lobs frozen kernels and butter.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>20/3s</color>
<color=#0077b6>Cryo System: </color><color=#8B0000>Attacks inflict Chill, add 10 Cryo points and deal ×4 damage to frozen zombies.</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Has a 25% chance to throw a frozen butter stick that freezes zombies for 4 seconds.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Cold Ice Mushroom + Jade Rice Thrower</color>

plants.name: Water Grass Sprout
plants.introduce: <color=#3D1400>Water Grass Sprout is extremely vigilant. It lives alone, foraging only in shallow waters. When necessary, it even hides beneath zombies to evade predators. It sleeps with one eye open—no easy feat for a plant with only one eye.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>When coming in contact with a zombie, Water Grass Sprout explodes and attaches itself to other zombies to multiply.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>20 × 30</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>When a zombie comes into contact with Water Grass Sprout, it will burst into 30 kelp projectiles that inflict 1 Tangle value before disappearing. If a zombie is killed by the kelp projectile, an extra Water Grass Sprout will spawn on that tile (if possible).</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Little Spray Mushroom > Tangle Surround Water Grass</color>

plants.name: Roll Heart Vegetable Squash Squash Thrower
plants.introduce: <color=#3D1400>"Smart robots are so handy! I customized one to my exact specifications, and it's massively boosted my throwing efficiency!" Roll Heart Vegetable Squash Squash Thrower exclaimed excitedly. "Oh, the battle is about to begin." With that, he leaped into a larger version of himself. Moments later, he heard the opponent say, "Smart robots are so handy! I customized one to..."</color>
plants.info:
Roll Heart Vegetable Squash Squash Thrower can launch tinier versions of itself, unmasking its layers to attack more zombies.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>60/3s (Squash Melon)</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Lobs a mini Roll Heart Vegetable Squash Squash Thrower that deals AOE crushing damage upon landing, then lobs 2 smaller Roll Heart Vegetable Squash Squash Throwers that deal 50% of the original damage before disappearing. The smaller Roll Heart Vegetable Squash Squash Throwers lob 2 even smaller Roll Heart Vegetable Squash Squash Throwers that deal 25% of the original damage and disappear immediately before disappearing itself.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Squash Melon + Roll Heart Vegetable Thrower</color>

plants.name: 3 Line Road Lantern Flower
plants.introduce: <color=#3D1400>3 Line Road Lantern Flower's favorite number is three. Triple the light, triple the vision, even triple the salary. With savings that generous, he can afford triple the snacks, neatly stored in his triple lamp cases. The triple things that he hates are triple the zombies, triple the waves and triple the gacha levels.</color>
plants.info:
Provides triple the Lumos of a normal Road Lantern Flower and splits lasers into three.

<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Provides 3 Lumos levels in a 3×3 area.
