<color=black>•</color> Flow Light Sage Person Palm and Flow Light Big Lantern Mushroom anti-ground lasers that pass through will create two additional lasers in the same lane, each dealing 30 damage</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>3 Line Shooter + Road Lantern Flower</color>

plants.name: Charm Confuse Flower Pot
plants.introduce: <color=#3D1400>Charm Confuse Flower Pot is well-liked by everyone. Plants, zombies, even mischievous little beetles are all her friends. She works at a mushroom nursery, and in her free time she's a proud member of the Zombuddies Club, often sharing her handmade rainbow clay during holidays.</color>
plants.info:
The charm within the pot nurtures hypnotized zombies.

<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Grants hypnotized zombies on its tile 33% Damage Reduction and heals 1 HP every 0.04s. When bitten, hypnotizes the attacker and reverts back to a Flower Pot.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Charm Confuse Mushroom > Flower Pot</color>

plants.name: Flow Light Double Shot Shooter
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>The weekly Pool Light Festival is an important social event for Flow Light Double Shot Shooter. Before heading out, it carefully adjusts its lamp hat, the leaves under the brim not only make it look stylish, but also give its firefly friends a place to rest after the party.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Fires two high-frequency lasers.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>10×2/0.2s</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Gains Lumos energy equal to Lumos level every 0.5s, up to 1000. Each laser beam consumes (1 + current energy × 10%) energy when attacking or transferring.
<color=black>•</color> Can lock onto a Flow Light Middle Continue Shooter in front in the same lane (not directly adjacent). Transfers energy every 0.1s, restoring energy equal to 20% of its attack. Each point of energy spent doubles the amount transferred.
<color=black>•</color> Consumes energy every 0.2s to attack 2 zombies at once, increasing damage by the amount of energy spent.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Pea Shooter + Flow Light Pea Shooter</color>

plants.name: Flow Light Middle Continue Shooter
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"It's my turn to wear the hat." "But the strap is under my chin."The two heads of the Lumen Relay Shooter argue constantly. Even the Pool Light Festival can't distract them - the only thing that stops the argument is when they start fighting over the eyebrows instead.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Receives laser energy from the left and fires high-frequency lasers.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>10/0.2s</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> The right head functions like a Flow Light Pea Shooter.
<color=black>•</color> The left head can receive Lumos energy from beamshooter-family plants in the same lane (not in directly adjacent tile).</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Pea Shooter + Flow Light Double Shot Shooter</color>

plants.name: Flow Light Machine Gun Shooter
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Flow Light Machine Gun Shooter shines brilliantly on the battlefield, often earning four times the credit. Though its glow makes it an easy target, it never backs down. Wounds within the light are a warrior's badge of honor.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Fires four high-frequency lasers.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>10×4/0.2s</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Gains Lumos energy equal to Lumos level every 0.5s, up to 1000. Each laser beam consumes (1 + current energy × 10%) energy when attacking or transferring.
<color=black>•</color> Can lock onto a Flow Light Middle Continue Shooter in front in the same lane (not directly adjacent). Transfers energy every 0.1s, restoring energy equal to 40% of its attack. Each point of energy spent doubles the amount transferred.
<color=black>•</color> Consumes energy every 0.2s to attack 4 zombies at once, increasing damage by the amount of energy spent.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Pea Shooter + Flow Light Middle Continue Shooter</color>

plants.name: Spicy Pepper Little Mushroom
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Each little Spicy Pepper Little Mushroom may be small and insignificant on its own, but together they pack serious heat. That's why they like to stick in groups. Other plants even rate them by size: mild, medium, and extra hot.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Small body, fiery temper.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>600 (Cremator)</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Short plant. Explodes when zombies get close, triggering a Fire Burst Spicy Pepper effect with less damage.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Fire Burst Spicy Pepper > Little Spray Mushroom</color>

plants.name: Garlic Poison Pea Shooter
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Despite the name, Garlic Poison Pea Shooter is a chef with a strong signature style. He insists every customer try its specialty, fried peas with garlic powder. The smell alone brings zombies flooding with memories: childhood, family, home… and plenty of tears.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Fires garlic peas that cause poisoned zombies to release toxins, forming a toxic cloud.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>30/1.5s</color>
<color=#5E1675>Poison System: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Projectiles add 1 Poison value to zombies on hit.
<color=black>•</color> If the zombie hit is already in a Poisoned state, creates a Toxic Cloud at the zombie's feet that lasts for 6s, applying 1 Poison value and dealing damage equal to 20× the total projectiles fired in one volley by all Poison-system straight-shooting plants in this lane per second to all zombies caught within. There can be up to 1 active Toxic Cloud per lane.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Pea Shooter + Big Garlic</color>

plants.name: Garlic Poison Double Shot Shooter
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"Fight! Fight! Attack! Attack!" Garlic Poison Double Shot Shooter lives with passion and poison. Nothing distracts it, not zombies, not vampires, not even pushy salesmen.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Fires two garlic peas at once.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>30×2/1.5s</color>
<color=#5E1675>Poison System: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Projectiles add 1 Poison value to zombies on hit.
<color=black>•</color> If the zombie hit is already in a Poisoned state, creates a Toxic Cloud at the zombie's feet.</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000> Tap to auto-fuse with a free Pea Shooter 15s after planting.
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Pea Shooter + Garlic Poison Pea Shooter</color>

plants.name: Garlic Poison Crack Pod Shooter
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Bouncing shots are a must-have skill for modern Crack Pod Shooters, but Garlic Poison Crack Pod Shooter did not bother to learn it. Nor does he had to. He can simply fire garlic peas into the house behind it, and sooner or later someone will throw them right back.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Fires garlic peas both forwards and backwards.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>30×3/1.5s</color>
<color=#5E1675>Poison System: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Projectiles add 1 Poison value to zombies on hit.
<color=black>•</color> If the zombie hit is already in a Poisoned state, creates a Toxic Cloud at the zombie's feet.</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000> Tap to auto-fuse with a free Pea Shooter 15s after planting.
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Pea Shooter + Garlic Poison Double Shot Shooter</color>

plants.name: Garlic Poison Machine Gun Shooter
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>At the neighborhood potluck, renowned linguist Garlic Poison Machine Gun Shooter proudly served a "traditional recipe" of 'pois' and 'poisson'. It tasted excellent, except everybody got food poisoning afterwards. To this day he still insists the 'poison' was part of the 'Gift'.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Fires four garlic peas at once.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>30×4/1.5s</color>
<color=#5E1675>Poison System: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Projectiles add 1 Poison value to zombies on hit.
<color=black>•</color> If the zombie hit is already in a Poisoned state, creates a Toxic Cloud at the zombie's feet.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Pea Shooter + Garlic Poison Crack Pod Shooter</color>

plants.name: Fire Torch Big Spray Mushroom
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Fire Torch Big Spray Mushroom made a name for himself by quickly cooking peas into rich pea paste and turning them into "pea tarts." His skills are famous, but so is his terrible business sense. His business goes bankrupt at the rate of one restaurant per year, mostly because he burns through far too many peas just to maintain his hairstyle.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Burns incoming peas, compressing them into a burst of flame.

<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Does not attack on its own.
<color=black>•</color> Absorbs passing peas. Every 1s, exhales a piercing flame 4 tiles forward. Damage equals the total damage of absorbed peas, up to 300.
<color=black>•</color> Melts Cold Ice Shooters into normal peas.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Big Spray Mushroom + Fire Torch Tree Stump</color>

plants.name: Big Mouth Squash Melon
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"So hungry, so hungry, so hungry! I'm starving!" Big Mouth Squash Melon shouts as he lunges at zombies ahead. No matter how much he eats, it's never enough. Oh look - a Gargantuar! Finally, a proper meal… Wait, that might've been too much.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Gluttonous Squash Melon that keeps squashing when fed.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>1800 (Squash Melon)</color>
<color=black>Detection Range: </color><color=#8B0000>1 tile to the right</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> On landing, attempts to devour one zombie caught underneath. Still deals damage to zombies that are not eaten.
<color=black>•</color> On a successful devour, returns to the field on the same tile, otherwise he disappears.</color>
plants.cost: <size=36>Fusion Recipe: <color=#8B0000>Big Mouth Flower + Squash Melon</color>

plants.name: Pea Towards Sun Flower (TD)
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"Actually, the Towards Sun Flower behind me is my sister," Pea Towards Sun Flower explained to everyone. "We've been inseparable since birth. She loves producing Sun, and I destroy zombies on her behalf." But everyone knows the truth: the cheerful Pea brother is a total siscon. After all, every zombie that gets near his sister mysteriously ends up vaporized by sunlight.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Pea Towards Sun Flower can fire Sun shots that occasionally produce Sun.

<color=blue>Reversible by pressing R</color>
<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>20/1.5s</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Every third shot drops 5 Sun when hitting targets.</color>
<color=black>Upgrades: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>• Basic Upgrade: </color>(80 Sun) Attack Damage & Speed +20%
<color=black>• Elite Upgrade Path 1: </color>(200 Sun) Attack Damage +200%
<color=black>• Elite Upgrade Path 2: </color>(200 Sun) Attack Speed +100%</color>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>200</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>7.5s</color></size>

plants.name: Sun Light Big Mushroom (TD)
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>A seasoned sunlight banker, Sun Light Big Mushroom offers high interest on your deposits."You chose a time deposit," he says sternly to a frustrated Sun Light Emperor Fruit. "Withdrawals aren't allowed just yet."</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>A visionary sun producer that absorbs and multiplies nearby sun, paying out in bursts.

<color=black>Sun Output: </color><color=#8B0000>25/25s</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Absorbs dropped Sun within a 5×5 area, multiplies their value by ×1.3, and stores them.
<color=black>•</color> Releases as much Sun as possible in batches of 25 along with the next production.</color>
<color=black>Upgrades: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>• Basic Upgrade: </color>(60 Sun) Sun Production Speed +50%
<color=black>• Elite Upgrade Path 1: </color>(150 Sun) Sun Production Speed +200%
<color=black>• Elite Upgrade Path 2: </color>(150 Sun) Sun Value Multiplier ×2</color>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>150</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>7.5s</color></size>

plants.name: Super Tier Cherry Peach Shooter (TD)
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Meet Super Tier Cherry Peach Shooter, the ultimate plant killer. He's more irritable than ever—who would've thought the warm and friendly Cherry Peach Shooter would swallow another special cherry? Don't push your luck with him. That cherry stuck in his throat might just be his next shot—his face is already turning red!</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Shoots out explosive cherries that explode on impact.

<color=blue>Reversible by pressing R</color>
<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>20/3s</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Immune to explosive cherries and deals AoE damage.</color>
<color=black>Upgrades: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>• Basic Upgrade: </color>(160 Sun) Attack Damage +50%
<color=black>• Elite Upgrade Path 1: </color>(400 Sun) Attack Damage +100%, Explosion Radius +50%
<color=black>• Elite Upgrade Path 2: </color>(400 Sun) Attack Speed +200%</color>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>400</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>50s</color></size>

plants.name: Foreign Peach Tough Fruit (TD)
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Foreign Peach Tough Fruit lives under a rock and rarely moves. It's not because he's lazy, but because he's got standards. He'll only act if invited by a certain square friend to jellyfishing… or to prank a cephalopodic neighbor.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>A reactive defender that counters with little stars.

<color=black>Toughness: </color><color=#8B0000>4000</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Fires five 10-damage stars in all directions when damaged.</color>
<color=black>Upgrades: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>• Basic Upgrade: </color>(80 Sun) HP and Max Toughness +50%
<color=black>• Elite Upgrade Path 1: </color>(200 Sun) HP +200%
<color=black>• Elite Upgrade Path 2: </color>(200 Sun) Regen +100% (100 HP/3s)</color>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>200</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>30s</color></size>

plants.name: Sun Light Earth Bean Thunder (TD)
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"Stop at red lights, slow down at yellow lights, and obey traffic rules!" Sun Light Earth Bean Thunder frequently warns zombies. But to the zombies, all they see is the disgusting green hue before them. He clutches his green leaves and pouts, wishing for more sunshine. "After all, sunshine is what fuels us all!"</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Sun Light Earth Bean Thunder produces Sun until and after its explosion.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>1000</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Drops 50 Sun on explosion.</color>
<color=black>Upgrades: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>• Basic Upgrade: </color>(10 Sun) Explosion Damage +50%
<color=black>• Elite Upgrade Path 1: </color>(25 Sun) Explosion Radius +50%
<color=black>• Elite Upgrade Path 2: </color>(25 Sun) Explosion Damage +100%</color>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>25</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>50s</color></size>

plants.name: Cold Ice Worry Gloom Mushroom (TD)
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Dreaming of a career as a physicist, the Cold Ice Worry Gloom Mushroom dedicates his days to unraveling the complexities of the world, avoiding contact with other plants. "Why do I have multiple strings of icicles sprouting from my head?" he muses, the latest question occupying his thoughts. He's oddly adamant about calling them "strings."</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Releases cold fumes around that can freeze zombies.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>5/3.8s (Piercing)</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Attacks apply Chill.</color>
<color=black>Upgrades: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>• Basic Upgrade: </color>(80 Sun) Attack Speed +50%
<color=black>• Elite Upgrade Path 1: </color>(200 Sun) Attacks add 10 Cryo points
<color=black>• Elite Upgrade Path 2: </color>(200 Sun) Chill Duration ×2</color>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>200</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>7.5s</color></size>

plants.name: Cherry Peach Big Mouth Flower (TD)
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Ever since her drink with Cherry Peach Explode Bullet, Big Mouth Flower has been constantly "buzzing." She turned into an absolute menace every meal. The sound of chewing transformed into explosive pops as plates and bowls flew everywhere, leaving everyone unwilling to dine with her. After a show of hands, Cherry Peach Big Mouth Flower ended up eating in the yard… at least she no longer has to buy new tableware.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Produces a cherry explosion upon eating a zombie.

<color=blue>Reversible by pressing R</color>
<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>Insta-kill (Devour)</color>
<color=black>Digestion Time: </color><color=#8B0000>40s</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Creates a 3×3 Cherry explosion with 1000 damage in the tile directly ahead on successful biting.</color>
<color=black>Upgrades: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>• Basic Upgrade: </color>(200 Sun) Explosion Damage +20%, Digestion Time -10s
<color=black>• Elite Upgrade Path 1: </color>(500 Sun) Explosion Damage +100%
<color=black>• Elite Upgrade Path 2: </color>(500 Sun) Digestion Time -50% (15s)</color>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>500</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>50s</color></size>

plants.name: Flash Lightning Ocean Leek (TD)
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Flash Lightning Ocean Leek thought his moment had come — after all, who else had eaten lightning over a hundred times? Surely, he was destined to play the Frankenstein monster! But when he received the script, his dreams short-circuited. He wasn't a star. He wasn't even a sidekick. He was just Dr. Stanley's lightning rod...</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Attacks approaching zombies with lightning rays!

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>20/1.5s</color>
<color=black>Attack Range: </color><color=#8B0000>5×5</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Idle Flash Lightning Ocean Leeks in range will channel power to the attacking one, each boosting the damage by 150%.
<color=black>•</color> Each shot can arc to other zombies to deal 1/3 the damage, up to a maximum of 3 zombies.</color>
<color=black>Upgrades: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>• Basic Upgrade: </color>(120 Sun) Attack Damage +50%
<color=black>• Elite Upgrade Path 1: </color>(300 Sun) Arcing now deals full damage
<color=black>• Elite Upgrade Path 2: </color>(300 Sun) Attack Damage + 300% but no longer arcs</color>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>300</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>7.5s</color></size>

plants.name: Pea Little Spray Mushroom (TD)
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"Don't let my size fool you; even a small body can contain a lot of energy," Pea Little Spray Mushroom cautions those who underestimate him. "We all began as tiny mushrooms, making progress a little at a time. With dedication to a sunny breakfast, you can become just like me."</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Pea Little Spray Mushroom shoots further than Little Spray Mushroom.

<color=blue>Reversible by pressing R</color>
<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>10/1.5s</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Short plant, stackable.</color>
<color=black>Upgrades: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>• Basic Upgrade: </color>(20 Sun) Attack Damage + 50%
<color=black>• Elite Upgrade Path 1: </color>(50 Sun) Attack Damage +100%, now fires ignited small peas
<color=black>• Elite Upgrade Path 2: </color>(50 Sun) Attack Damage +200%</color>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>50</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>7.5s</color></size>

plants.name: Sun Light Mushroom (TD)
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Sun Light Mushroom hates sun. He hates it so much that when it builds up in his system, he spits it out as fast as he can. He just won't abide it. To him, sun is crass.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Sun Light Mushrooms give small sun at first and normal sun later.

<color=black>Sun Output: </color><color=#8B0000>5→15/25s</color>
<color=black>Growth Time: </color><color=#8B0000>120s</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Short plant, stackable.</color>
<color=black>Upgrades: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>• Basic Upgrade: </color>(10 Sun) Sun Production Time → 16.67s
<color=black>• Elite Upgrade Path 1: </color>(25 Sun) Grows immediately and produces Sun
<color=black>• Elite Upgrade Path 2: </color>(25 Sun) Now produces 2 Suns at once</color>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>25</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>7.5s</color></size>

plants.name: Destroy Eradicate Big Spray Mushroom (TD)
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Destroy Eradicate Big Spray Mushroom, the misanthropic workaholic, can't stand the sight of zombies—too much of a distraction. He's got a small stash of bullets, but they're more than lethal enough. "Don't bother me unless it's urgent. If you want them dead, just shoot them yourself." Still, his sharp efficiency keeps everyone looking up to him.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Destroy Eradicate Big Spray Mushroom can destroy most zombies in front of it.

<color=blue>Reversible by pressing R</color>
<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>1000/60s</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Click manually in the Upgrade Menu to attack all zombies in the row in front.</color>
<color=black>Upgrades: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>• Basic Upgrade: </color>(200 Sun) Attack Damage +50%
<color=black>• Elite Upgrade Path 1: </color>(500 Sun) Attack Damage +200%
<color=black>• Elite Upgrade Path 2: </color>(500 Sun) Attack CD -50%</color>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>500</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>50s</color></size>

plants.name: Grave Tomb Swallow Consumer (TD)
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Despite Grave Tomb Swallow Consumer's fearsome appearance, he wants everyone to know that he loves kittens and spends his off hours volunteering at a local zombie rehabilitation center. "It's just the right thing to do," he says.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Plant Grave Tomb Swallow Consumers on statues to remove the statues.

<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Can bust Statues.</color>
<color=black>Sun Statue Mechanic: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Statues block straight-shooting projectiles from plants and have 1000 HP each.
<color=black>•</color> Statues can only be damaged by projectiles or Grave Tomb Swallow Consumers.
<color=black>•</color>Towards Sun Flower Statues drop 100 Sun when destroyed, Sun Light Mushroom Statues drop 50.</color>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>0</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>50s</color></size>

plants.name: Big Courage Mushroom (TD)
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"Courage fills me, no fear remains~ Whether it's spring, fall, winter, or summer~" Big Courage Mushroom no longer shrinks in the face of zombies. "I used to tremble, but Senior Big Spray Mushroom told me, 'If a zombie gets near, spray your spores. Hiding won't solve anything.'"</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Big Courage Mushroom is ready to take on the zombies, near or far!

<color=blue>Reversible by pressing R</color>
<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>20×2/1.5s (Long range); 20/1.5s (Short range, piercing)</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Shoots 2 spores at once. When zombies get within 4 tiles in range, sprays piercing fumes instead.</color>
<color=black>Upgrades: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>• Basic Upgrade: </color>(80 Sun) Attack Damage & Speed +20%
<color=black>• Elite Upgrade Path 1: </color>(200 Sun) Attack Damage +200%, now permanently shoots spores
<color=black>• Elite Upgrade Path 2: </color>(200 Sun) Attack Damage +100%, range for spraying fumes ×2</color>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>200</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>7.5s</color></size>

plants.name: Cold Ice 3 Leaf Grass (TD)
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Cold Ice 3 Leaf Grass is a -4°C air conditioner. When his chilly breeze blows, it feels like stepping straight into winter. Even Cold Ice Shooter is frozen, the cold wind actually froze his own projectiles. What could possibly be colder than that? Answer: The atmosphere after Extreme Cold Ice Bean's told one of his totally tone deaf jokes.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>3 Leaf Grass of a freezing breeze that constantly applies chill.

<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Lasts for 10s before disappearing.
<color=black>•</color> Deals 1 damage and applies 20 Cryo points to all zombies on the lawn.
<color=black>•</color> Converts all snow peas on screen to polar ice peas.</color>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>125</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>50s</color></size>

plants.name: Destroy Eradicate Foreign Peach (TD)
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"I could destroy almost the entire planet if I wanted to," boasts Destroy Eradicate Foreign Peach, "and they'd all blame it on the biggest star in the sky..."</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Transforms himself into a Doom Meteor and crashes into the ground.

<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Disappears after 3s and summons a Doom Meteor that deals 1800 area damage on impact and splits into 180 spore stars each dealing 20 damage.</color>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>175</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>90s</color></size>

plants.name: Pig Cage Grass Mortar Strike Artillery (TD)
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>"Coach, I want to play basketball." Drippy Pitcher has always dreamed of becoming a basketball star. He practices shooting day and night without rest. He has two advantages: first, he kind of looks like a basketball hoop; second, he has an endless supply of basketballs to practice with. For heaven's sake, don't ask what those balls are made of.</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>Lobs droplet shells that bounce between the zombie and other Drippy Pitchers.

<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>40/3s</color>
<color=black>Range: </color><color=#8B0000>Within 2 tiles in all four directions</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Projectiles rebound to nearby other Drippy Pitchers on hit, prompting them to immediately attack twice.</color>
<color=black>Upgrades: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>• Basic Upgrade: </color>(120 Sun) Attack Damage & Speed +20%
<color=black>• Elite Upgrade Path 1: </color>(300 Sun) Attack Damage +200%
<color=black>• Elite Upgrade Path 2: </color>(300 Sun) Attack Speed +100%, projectiles can now rebound to self</color>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>300</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>7.5s</color></size>

plants.name: Sleep Lily (TD)
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Sleep Lily never complains. Sleep Lily never wants to know what's going on. Put a plant on top of Sleep Lily, he won't say a thing. Does he have startling opinions or shocking secrets? Nobody knows. Sleep Lily keeps it all inside.</color></size>
plants.info:
<size=36>Sleep Lilys let you plant non-aquatic plants on top of them.

<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>Short plant. Non-aquatic plants can be planted on it.</color></size>
plants.cost:
<size=36>Cost: <color=#8B0000>25</color>
Recharge: <color=#8B0000>7.5s</color></size>

plants.name: Squash Melon Tough Fruit (TD)
plants.introduce: <align=left><size=36><color=black>Dragged from a kids' playground by his stem and dumped on the lawn, Squash Melon Tough Fruit was rejected for being a deformed rubber ball. Now, furious and bumpy, he bounces wildly, yelling: "I'm gonna play all I want! ALL I WANT!"</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>A bouncy nut who's all tantrum and impact.

<color=black>Toughness: </color><color=#8B0000>1000</color>
<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>80/1.5s (Squash Melon)</color>

plants.name: 3 Line Earth Bean Thunder (TD)
plants.introduce:
<align=left><size=36><color=black>3 Line Earth Bean Thunder loves playing tricks underground. It shoots out surprises not once, not twice, but thrice—like a cheeky prankster with a never-ending stash of boom. Watch your step, or you might get a triple whammy that’ll blow your boots off.

- Written by Mrs. Draco</color>
plants.info:
<size=36>3 Line Earth Bean Thunder resurfaces after exploding and fires potatoes across three lanes.

<color=blue>Reversible by pressing R</color>
<color=black>Damage: </color><color=#8B0000>20×3/1.5s</color>
<color=black>Special: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>•</color> Short plant (evades straight-firing projectiles).
<color=black>•</color> When armed, explodes on contact, dealing 3600 damage, knocks back for 1 tile and retreats into the ground.
<color=black>•</color> Re-emerges 15s after exploding. Explodes and disappears immediately upon contact with vehicles.</color>
<color=black>Upgrades: </color><color=#8B0000>
<color=black>• Basic Upgrade: </color>(160 Sun) Projectile Damage +50%
<color=black>• Elite Upgrade Path 1: </color>(400 Sun) Projectile Damage +200%
<color=black>• Elite Upgrade Path 2: </color>(400 Sun) Now unleashes an Ultimate move every 60s, firing 250 shots straight ahead over 5s, plus 500 more in a 0-45° spread</color>

Recharge: <color=#8B0000>30s</color></size>
